Browse NIP-99 listings, place an order, pay the Lightning invoice, receive credentials — and run the browser-feasible subset of the service-management `ops` interface — all in the browser.

## Why
The two-sided marketplace needs a website, not just a CLI. A WASM client reuses the one Rust protocol implementation (no duplicate JS protocol), and static hosting preserves the no-central-server property (§1): the site is a pure buyer front-end over relays + the buyer's wallet. The same site should also let a buyer manage what they rented (ADR-0013), at least for request/response ops. This is the **human** surface (ADR-0014): agents use the buyer CLI (lnrent-7fp.13), not this SPA, and the SPA stays static — it does NOT expose an HTTP/agent API (that would reintroduce a server and break the no-central-server property).

## Scope
wasm32 build of buyer-core (wasm-pack/trunk); a minimal SPA (browse listings, order form, invoice view, creds view, and a service-management view); browser glue — NIP-07 (window.nostr) signer, and an **invoice hand-off to the user's wallet** (present the bolt11 to `window.webln` / show QR / copy) with NO payer trait and NO autonomous client payment — the SPA never pays, the user's wallet does (ADR-0014, §4.7); relays over browser WebSockets; the `request`-kind ops subset (op.request/op.result over the same buyer-core); static hosting.

## Non-goals
Operator/seller UI (operator side stays daemon/CLI/skills); a hosted backend OR an HTTP/agent API (must stay static — agents use the CLI, ADR-0014); embedded custodial wallet; the VM access/reachability plane (separate from buying); `interactive`-kind ops (Iroh shell/console/logs -f, §9.2) — out of browser scope for M1a (the buyer-core seam exists but the web shell does not wire an Iroh transport).

## Critical constraints / risks
The rust-nostr-on-wasm32 gift-wrap gate is already PROVEN (the .19 codec spike + flake.nix; build inside `nix develop`). NIP-07 / WebLN need a compatible extension/wallet -> ship the copy-bolt11/QR fallback. No secrets in the static bundle.

## Dependencies
Depends on: buyer-core + CLI buyer (lnrent-7fp.13) and a working loop to test against (lnrent-7fp.15, which also gates the operator-side op dispatch). Follows the CLI buyer.

## Verification
A headless-browser e2e drives the SPA through the full loop, including a request-kind management op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §1, §3, §4.7 (human surface, no agent API; invoice hand-off), §5.1, §7.4, §9.2, §14; ADR-0013, ADR-0014; eng-review (web client, formerly M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The SPA loads as static files (no backend) and connects to relays over browser WebSockets.
- A headless-browser test completes the full buyer loop: browse a listing -> order.request -> the SPA presents the invoice and hands it off to the user's wallet (a MOCKED `window.webln` extension settles it out-of-band — the SPA itself does not pay) -> receive provision.ready -> display credentials.
- Signing uses NIP-07 (embedded-key fallback, export-prompted since it decrypts creds); the invoice is handed to the user's wallet via WebLN, else shown as copy-bolt11 / QR — the SPA holds no wallet credentials and runs no payer.
- A buyer with NEITHER NIP-07 nor WebLN completes the loop via embedded key + copy-bolt11/QR hand-off, paying from their own phone wallet (the headless-browser test covers this fallback path).
- The SPA runs a `request`-kind management op (e.g. status) on an active subscription and displays op.result.data.
- buyer-core is shared with the CLI (lnrent-7fp.13) -- no duplicated protocol logic.

## Capability degradation (grill)
Detect and degrade: signing prefers NIP-07, else an embedded key the SPA generates + persists (and prompts to export/back up, since it decrypts credentials); the invoice hand-off prefers WebLN, else copy-bolt11/QR — either way the user's own wallet pays, the SPA never does. A buyer with no Nostr extension and no WebLN wallet — just a phone Lightning wallet — can still complete the loop.
close_reason: DONE + acceptance proven: web WASM buyer (clients/web) built across 5 steps (scaffold+Clock 2ae0e1a, relay+?Send seam bba7a5c, signer+flows 0ce3977, static shell 5ee4ca4, dev-settle 1fb9848 + headless e2e). Both acceptance scenarios GREEN in real headless Chrome: full loop (connect->discover->order->WebLN handoff->dev-settle->credentials->request-kind op) AND the no-NIP-07/no-WebLN QR/copy fallback. Reuses buyer-core (no protocol dup), static-hosted, SPA never pays.

title: lnrent wire-codec lib (DM types + gift-wrap + 30402)
description:
## Outcome
A shared, target-agnostic Rust lib for the lnrent wire protocol: the DM message types (order.request + renew.request carrying a client `id`; order.invoice, order.error + billing.invoice carrying the matching `request_id`; provision.ready, delivery.resend.request, billing.notice, billing.refund, sub.cancel, op.request, op.result) as typed structs with JSON ser/de; NIP-17 gift-wrap (NIP-59/44) encode/decode of those messages; and the NIP-99 (30402) listing build/parse, INCLUDING the recipe's published operation declarations (name/label/kind/params — NOT `hook`) so a buyer can discover the ops surface from a listing (§5.4, §7.4). Pure codec, no I/O. Compiles native AND wasm32. This bead PINS the on-wire schema (§5.4 listing layout + DM payloads, incl. op.request `id` and op.result `request_id`).

## Why
Both the OPERATOR Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5) and the BUYER buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13) must speak the exact same wire format — including the service-management op.request/op.result pair (§7.4, ADR-0013). Owning it once stops operator/buyer drift and removes the wrong coupling of buyer-core depending on the operator's daemon engine. wasm32 support lets the web buyer (lnrent-7fp.18) reuse it.

## Scope
message-type structs + JSON ser/de (incl. op.request/op.result); gift-wrap encode/decode (rust-nostr NIP-59/44); 30402 listing build/parse (incl. the recipe's `[[operation]]` declarations — name/label/kind/params); stable per-message id for idempotent dedupe. Pure functions — no relay/network/signer/payment I/O (those are seams in the consumers).

## Non-goals
Relay connections, signing, payment (consumers inject those); operator manifest (M5). The op DISPATCH logic (auth, running hooks) is the operator-side bead lnrent-7fp.20 and the buyer-side bead lnrent-7fp.13 — this bead is only the typed op.request/op.result codec they share.

## Constraints / risks
Must compile to wasm32 — the EARLIEST place to run the rust-nostr gift-wrap-on-wasm gate (also gates lnrent-7fp.18). **PROVEN (2026-06-24):** nostr (nip44/nip59) + secp256k1-sys + getrandom/js compiles for wasm32 in the nix devshell (flake.nix); secp's C cross-compile needs an unwrapped clang (CC_wasm32_unknown_unknown). Build wasm inside `nix develop`.

## Dependencies
None (root). Unblocks: operator Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5), buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13), operator op dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20).

## Verification
Unit: encode->decode round-trips identically for every message type (including op.request/op.result), including through NIP-17 gift-wrap; a 30402 listing builds and parses; the crate builds for native AND wasm32.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.3, §5.4 (listing op-declaration schema), §7.4; ADR-0009, ADR-0013.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every DM message type round-trips through JSON and through NIP-17 gift-wrap (encode->decode == identity), including op.request (with `id`) and the two error carriers — op.result and order.error — which use the SAME nested `error{code,message,retryable}` shape (NOT a top-level code), so agents branch uniformly (ADR-0014). The codec pins one shape; it does not infer both.
- A NIP-99 30402 listing builds and parses (the `listing_id` is the addressable coordinate `30402:<pubkey>:<d>`, §5.4), and round-trips the recipe's published operation declarations (name/label/kind/params; `hook` is never serialized into a listing) — build->parse == identity, with a schema `version` field and unknown-field tolerance.
- The crate compiles for native AND wasm32 (no native-only deps).
- Each message carries a stable id for idempotent dedupe; op.result.request_id correlates to the op.request id.
close_reason: lnrent-wire codec implemented (wire/ crate): typed NIP-17 DM messages + JSON serde, NIP-59/44 gift-wrap over a NostrSigner seam, NIP-99 30402 listing build/parse with op declarations. nostr 0.44; compiles native AND wasm32 (verified from source in the nix devshell); 30 tests. Built via rb-lite (8-round claude,codex implement/review loop, panel clean) + final codex xhigh review GO (P2 outer-wrap verification + 2 P3s fixed). Commits 23ca4b1/bf8ac4a.

title: Injectable clock for deterministic time
description:
## Outcome
An injectable time source (a Clock trait with now() returning unix seconds) used everywhere deadlines/timestamps are computed, so every time-based transition is deterministic under test.

## Why
The reconcile-loop state machine, invoice expiry, paid_through/soft_date, and downtime credit all key on wall-clock time. Without an injectable clock none of those paths are unit-testable.

## Scope
A Clock trait + a real SystemClock + a TestClock (settable/advanceable). Wire store and reconcile to take a Clock.

## Non-goals
Monotonic-vs-wall subtleties beyond the NTP assumption (a §16 minor risk).

## Dependencies
None (foundational root). Unblocks: store (lnrent-7fp.1), reconcile, and every time-based test.

## Verification
Unit: TestClock advance drives a fake deadline transition without sleeping.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.5; eng-review T11.

## Acceptance Criteria
- TestClock can set and advance time; a reconcile deadline fires under TestClock with no real sleep.
- SystemClock returns wall-clock unix seconds.
- store and reconcile take a Clock by injection (no direct std time calls in those paths).

title: Buyer management-op dispatch (op.request -> recipe hook -> op.result)
description:
## Outcome
Operator-side dispatch of buyer-facing management operations (SPEC.md §7.4, ADR-0013). The daemon receives an `op.request` NIP-17 DM `{id, subscription_id, op, params}`, authorizes it, runs the recipe's declared `request`-kind operation hook, and returns `op.result {request_id, status, data|error}`. This is the operator half of the unified buyer `ops` interface; the buyer half is buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13).

## Why
A buyer must manage the service they rented (WireGuard get-config, Fedimint admin-url/dkg, Hermes config/exec, and stop/restart/status everywhere) through ONE interface, without a per-service client and without arbitrary host shell. Recipes declare a narrow management surface; the daemon dispatches it deterministically. AI-free: every op runs a recipe hook, never an LLM (ADR-0013, §4.1).

## Scope
- Inbound `op.request` handling on top of the Nostr engine's DM primitives (lnrent-7fp.5), decoded via the wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19).
- **Authorization**: load the subscription via the store actor (lnrent-7fp.1); accept only if the DM sender pubkey == the subscription's `buyer_pubkey`, the op is declared by the subscription's recipe, and the subscription state permits it (M1a: ACTIVE only; suspended/terminated refuse — a per-op "available-when-suspended" manifest flag is a post-M1a refinement, not built here).
- **Durable idempotency (CRITICAL, codex)**: before running, upsert an `op_invocation` row keyed unique on `(sender_pubkey, request_id)` (the op.request `id`) via the store actor (lnrent-7fp.1). A declared op may be NON-idempotent (e.g. `restart`), so a duplicate `op.request` — NIP-17 redelivery, buyer retry after a dropped `op.result`, or daemon restart mid-op — MUST resend the cached (DONE/ERROR) or in-flight (RUNNING) result and MUST NOT re-run the hook. `op.result.request_id` correlates the reply.
- **Dispatch (request-kind only)**: resolve `Recipe::operation(op)`, run its `ops/<hook>` via the recipe runner (lnrent-7fp.6) with the lifecycle-hook I/O contract (JSON stdin: subscription + instance + validated op params + host facts; JSON stdout = `op.result.data`), run OFF the reconcile loop. The `hook` is a bare name resolved to `ops/<hook>`; reject any non-bare hook (traversal) — `Operation::hook_is_safe` / `Recipe::validate` (lnrent-7fp.6) is the gate.
- **Structured errors**: on failure return `op.result {status:"error", error:{code, message, retryable}}` with no `data`. `code` ∈ `unauthorized | unknown_op | invalid_params | not_active | timeout | hook_failed`; cap/redact hook stderr; an `unauthorized` reply must NOT reveal whether the `subscription_id` exists (someone else's sub and a nonexistent sub are indistinguishable to a non-buyer).
- Emit `op.result` back over NIP-17; the `op_invocation` row IS the durable audit record (op, subscription, sender, status, cached result/error).
- M1a proves this with the trivial recipe's minimal op(s) (`status` idempotent, `restart` non-idempotent — exercises the dedupe path).

## Non-goals
`interactive`-kind ops (shell / console / `logs -f` / file copy over the Iroh Native-connect session, §9.2) — those arrive with reachability (M1b+). Rich per-service ops (WireGuard get-config, Fedimint dkg, Hermes exec) ship with their recipes (M1b/M2). The buyer-side ops surface is lnrent-7fp.13.

## Constraints / failure handling
Op-param validation mirrors order-param validation (reject unknown/missing). A hook timeout/nonzero exit -> `op.result {status:error}`, never a daemon wedge (reuse the recipe runner's timeout + output-cap). Unauthorized or unknown-op requests get `op.result {status:error}` WITHOUT running any hook, and are logged. Op idempotency is keyed on `(sender_pubkey, op.request.id)` in `op_invocation` (NOT transport message-id dedupe): DONE/ERROR resends the cached result, a live RUNNING attaches to the in-flight task. **Startup recovery:** an `op_invocation` left `RUNNING` by a crash mid-op has no live task, so on daemon start it is converted to a terminal `error {code:"interrupted", retryable:false}` (effect unknown — neither re-run nor reported success); a later duplicate resends that, and the buyer may reissue under a NEW `id`.

## Dependencies
Depends on: wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19, for op.request `id` / op.result `request_id`), Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5, inbound DM + reply primitives), recipe runner (lnrent-7fp.6, runs the op hook + validates operation declarations incl. hook-name safety), store actor (lnrent-7fp.1, `op_invocation` idempotency + subscription lookup for auth). Unblocks: the e2e op proof in lnrent-7fp.15.

## Verification
Integration over a local relay + the trivial recipe: an authorized `op.request` runs the hook and returns `op.result`; a DUPLICATE op.request (same sender+id) resends the cached result and the hook runs exactly once (proven with non-idempotent `restart`); an `op.request` from a non-buyer pubkey is refused without running the hook and without leaking subscription existence; an undeclared op name is refused; a hook that times out / exits nonzero returns a structured `op.result` error.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1 (op.request/op.result, durable idempotency), §7.4, §11 (op_invocation); ADR-0013; ADR-0001 (daemon sole writer), ADR-0002 (recipes trusted code).

## Acceptance Criteria
- An authorized `op.request {id, subscription_id, op:"status", params:{}}` runs the trivial recipe's `ops/status` and returns `op.result {request_id, status:"ok", data:...}`.
- A duplicate `op.request` (same sender_pubkey + `id`) returns the SAME cached `op.result` and the hook runs exactly once — verified with non-idempotent `restart`.
- An invocation left `RUNNING` by a crash mid-op is recovered at startup to a terminal `error {code:"interrupted", retryable:false}` WITHOUT re-running; a duplicate then resends that cached error (tested).
- An `op.request` whose DM sender != the subscription's `buyer_pubkey` is refused with `op.result {status:"error", error.code:"unauthorized"}`, runs NO hook, and does not reveal whether the subscription exists.
- An `op.request` naming an op the recipe does not declare is refused (`unknown_op`, no hook run); a hook with a non-bare (traversal) path is rejected by validation, never executed.
- A management hook that exceeds its timeout or exits nonzero yields a structured `op.result` error (`timeout` / `hook_failed`) and does not wedge the reconcile loop.
- Every invocation is recorded durably in `op_invocation` (op, subscription, sender pubkey, state, cached result/error).
- M1a scope: only `request`-kind ops, ACTIVE subscriptions only; interactive/Iroh ops and the suspended-op `allowed_states` flag are explicitly out of scope here.
close_reason: Implemented in daemon/src/op_dispatch.rs (master, OpDispatch impl OpHandler): durable idempotency on (sender_pubkey, request_id) via op_invocation — claim RUNNING before the recipe hook, hook OUTSIDE the txn, commit DONE/ERROR after; duplicate resends the cached op.result (labeled with the STORED sub/op) and never re-runs the hook (proven with non-idempotent restart); concurrent RUNNING defers (no in-flight registry); recover_interrupted_ops flips orphaned RUNNING->interrupted at startup (for .21). Auth: sender==buyer_pubkey + ACTIVE + recipe-declared request-kind op; unauthorized/not_active/unknown_op/invalid_params return a structured op.result error, no hook, no existence leak. Hook stdin mirrors the lifecycle-hook contract (subscription+instance+params+host). Hook failures cached as op.result errors with redacted buyer messages, never a wedge. 8 tests; full suite+clippy green. Built via watched rb-lite (loop codex was blind to the untracked file; claude verified sound) + 2 independent codex gates (1st found 3: instance-context P1 + cached-metadata P2 fixed, is_timeout P3 + test; 2nd: no remaining P0/P1). Deferred: lnrent-c95 (in-flight RUNNING-attach, blocked by .21), lnrent-xjn (op_invocation/inbound_request retention/GC). Production wiring (run_inbound injection + startup recovery) is .21.

title: Daemon runtime wiring + task supervisor
description:
## Outcome
The production `lnrentd` main(): construct every subsystem and spawn the supervised concurrent tasks that make the money path actually run. Today daemon/src/main.rs only opens the store, loads recipes, and serves operator IPC — nothing wires the Nostr engine, payment watch, capture, provision, outbox, reconcile, refund, or op-dispatch together, so there is no runnable end-to-end daemon (codex next-steps review gap).

## Why
Each integrator bead (.5/.8/.10/.9/.11/.17/.20) owns one piece but not the assembly. The e2e ship gate (.15) needs a single runnable process that boots them all with one config + one operator key and keeps the long-lived loops alive. Without this bead .15 would secretly absorb a large, untested integration deliverable.

## Scope
- Load operator config + account-0 key (.16); open the store (.1); select the payment backend (MockPayment for M1a, .4 later).
- Construct the Nostr engine (.5) with the real signer + relays, injecting the order handler (.17) and op handler (.20) and the Outbound reply primitive.
- Spawn + SUPERVISE the long-lived tasks (restart on failure, structured shutdown): Nostr inbound routing (.5), payment watch -> capture (.8), provision + outbox sender (.10), reconcile loop (.9), refund executor (.11). On startup run the durable recovery each loop needs (re-drive PROVISIONING, drain unsent outbox, retry PENDING refunds, ERROR orphaned RUNNING op_invocations).
- Graceful shutdown: stop accepting, let in-flight commit, flush.

## Non-goals
The business logic inside each subsystem (their own beads). The e2e test itself (.15).

## Dependencies
Depends on the integrators it assembles: .16, .17, .10, .9, .11, .20 (and the already-done .5/.8/.1/.12). Unblocks: .15 ship gate.

## Verification
A smoke test: boot the wired daemon against an in-process relay + MockPayment + the dummy recipe, confirm it publishes a listing and a full order.request -> ... -> provision.ready completes; kill + restart mid-flight and confirm recovery (the durable loops re-drive). The exhaustive money-path assertions live in .15.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `lnrentd` boots from one config + the operator account-0 key (.16), selects MockPayment for M1a, and serves operator IPC AND the Nostr engine concurrently.
- The supervised loops (Nostr inbound, payment-watch->capture, provision+outbox, reconcile, refund) are all spawned; a panic/error in one restarts it without taking down the process.
- On startup the durable recovery runs: PROVISIONING re-driven, unsent outbox drained, PENDING refunds retried, orphaned RUNNING op_invocations marked ERROR.
- Smoke test: against an in-process relay + MockPayment + the dummy recipe, the daemon publishes its listing and a full order.request -> order.invoice -> (pay) -> provision.ready completes; killing the daemon mid-handshake and restarting recovers (no lost/duplicated effect).
- Graceful shutdown drains in-flight work and flushes; `cargo test` + `cargo clippy` clean.
close_reason: Implemented daemon/src/supervisor.rs + main.rs wiring (master ca38be2): constructs every subsystem from .16 config + account-0 key, selects MockPayment (fails on fedimint), runs the money path as a supervised daemon. Boot recovery (recover_interrupted_ops -> provision recover -> settlement catch-up [scans OPEN order+renewal, lookup, capture-if-Paid, conservative settle-time capped at the binding boundary incl. renewal paid_through+retention_s] -> refunder.drive -> reconcile catch-up -> outbox drain). Supervised loops (IPC, Nostr inbound, settlement watch->capture, reconcile, maintenance) under a restart supervisor (capped-backoff restart that resets after a healthy run; shared shutdown; draining IPC+handler wrappers). MockPayment.set_now seeded+synced to the daemon clock. Listing published+upserted. Graceful shutdown drains+flushes. Smoke test boots the wired daemon over an in-process relay + MockPayment + dummy recipe, proving order->invoice->settle->provision.ready + crash/restart recovery. Full suite (192 unit + supervisor smoke) + clippy green. Built via watched rb-lite (recovered from a double-run mishap to a clean base) + 1 codex gate that caught a renewal retention-boundary settle-cap P1 (fixed) + no remaining P0/P1. Deferred: complete nostr-inbound shutdown drain -> lnrent-k82 (needs a nostr_engine API; durability covers it for M1a).

title: Downtime credit on restart (heartbeat + outage-window deadline shift)
description:
## Outcome
On restart, CREDIT operator downtime so a buyer is never suspended for the operator's outage
(ADR-0005 revision; home-lab boxes reboot). Persist a heartbeat (the `daemon_state` row) on the
reconcile tick. On restart, compute the outage window `[last_heartbeat, now]`; for any subscription
whose suspend/destroy deadline (`next_deadline`) fell INSIDE that window, SHIFT it forward by the
outage duration and re-send its reminder. A deadline OUTSIDE the window is NOT shifted.

## Why
Operator boxes reboot; without downtime credit a buyer gets suspended for the OPERATOR's outage, not
their own non-payment. Split out of the core reconcile bead (.9) because it is a distinct
restart-resilience feature, not part of the mock money-path demo — and it was where the un-bounded
.9 attempt accumulated much of its complexity.

## Scope
heartbeat persist on the tick; the restart outage-window computation + deadline shift + reminder
re-send. Re-key safely: shifting a deadline must NOT orphan an already-issued renewal invoice keyed
on the old paid_through and re-issue a DUPLICATE auto-renewal invoice (a real bug the .9 review
found, esp. for renew_lead_s == 0, where the reminder marker keys on paid_through) — one cycle still
yields exactly one invoice (§6.6).

## Non-goals
The core reconcile tick + deadline transitions (.9); settlement (.8); refund (.11).

## Dependencies
Depends on: the core reconcile (.9). Does NOT block the e2e ship gate (.15) — downtime credit is
M1a-resilience, not the mock money-path demo (like .14/.18).

## Acceptance Criteria (unit, TestClock)
- A suspend deadline that fell in the recorded outage window is SHIFTED by the outage; one OUTSIDE
  the window is NOT shifted; missed deadlines fire on restart.
- The deadline shift does NOT re-issue a duplicate renewal invoice (the renew_lead_s==0 double-issue
  guard): one cycle, one invoice.

## Spec refs
ADR-0005 (+ revision); SPEC §6.5.
close_reason: Downtime credit on restart DONE (master, daemon/src/{reconcile,capture,order_intake,supervisor,store}.rs + docs): a nullable subscription.suspend_not_before floor set on restart for ACTIVE subs whose renewal window overlapped the outage [last_heartbeat,now], giving the buyer their full renew_lead window of operator-availability before suspension. paid_through NEVER moved (prepaid + renew:auto invoice anchor) -> renewal math + dup-invoice guard trivial; floor self-expires + cleared on renewal. The credited boundary B = max(paid_through,suspend_not_before)+retention_s is honored uniformly by reconcile (suspend/destroy/auto-invoice-expiry), capture (refund gate), order_intake (renew.request/load_renewable), and the .21 settlement catch-up. Boot recovery credits BEFORE catch-up; heartbeat monotonic; credit path mints no invoices (backend-down-at-boot can't lose credit). Comprehensively unit-tested (interior/repeat/long outage, soft-date-only, zero-lead, no-op, boot order, cross-module consistency); full suite + clippy green; codex gate = no P0/P1. Designed with the user after the first cut revealed the cascade (capture+order_intake+catch-up all consume the boundary). Deferred: SUSPENDED-subs' destroy credit (lnrent-d6n); polish nits (post-reminder auto-invoice expiry refresh, migration hygiene) (lnrent-ux7) — no money/correctness bugs. ADR-0005 + SPEC §6.5/§11 updated to the floor design.

title: sqlite migrations (user_version)
description:
## Outcome
A minimal schema-version/migration mechanism (sqlite user_version) so the schema can evolve past M0 without a painful first column change.

## Why
store.rs currently applies CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with no versioning; the first real migration would be ad hoc. Codex flagged this.

## Scope
A migrations runner keyed on PRAGMA user_version; M1a is version 1 (the current §11 schema).

## Non-goals
Down-migrations.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: any later schema change.

## Verification
Unit: opening an old (v0) db migrates to v1; opening a current db is a no-op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §11; eng-review T12; codex finding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opening a fresh db sets user_version = 1 and applies the §11 schema.
- Opening a current (v1) db is a no-op.
- Unit test: a simulated v0 db migrates to v1 without data loss.

title: Fedimint PaymentBackend (ecash via existing federation + gateway)
description:
## Outcome
A Fedimint-backed PaymentBackend — the PRIMARY receive path (ADR-0012). The operator's fedimint client (member of an EXISTING federation, routing through an EXISTING gatewayd) issues a gateway bolt11 bound to an external_id, receives into ecash, and exposes the §6.1 trait: create_invoice(amount, memo, expiry, external_id) (idempotent on external_id), lookup, pay(dest, amount, idempotency_key) (ecash-out refund via the gateway, idempotent on the key, returns a payment id), payment_status / payment_status_by_key (returning the OUTBOUND `PayStatus`, distinct from invoice `PaymentStatus`), and watch() — a settlement stream emitting ecash-received for an invoice's external_id.

## Why
Fedimint ecash is the economic fit for low-value rentals: NO per-payment inbound-liquidity cost (the gateway eats the LN side), unlike phoenixd whose first payment triggers a costly channel open (ADR-0012). phoenixd + LND are later secondary backends (M3). Fedimint cannot hold invoices either, so capture-then-refund applies (ADR-0003).

## Dependencies + DB backend (ADR-0015)
Adds **`fedimint-client 0.11.1` + `fedimint-rocksdb`** to the DAEMON crate (pinned 0.11.1 = the operator's federation version). The fedimint client's own state lives in **RocksDB** (its only published persistent backend) — a SECOND DB engine alongside our sqlite store (§11), which the client owns and we never touch directly. The daemon ships **glibc-dynamic** (musl-static dropped — RocksDB's C++ on musl is painful; a custom sqlite IRawDatabase is a documented later option). `fedimint-client` is DAEMON-ONLY — never in the wasm buyer-core (payment out of scope for the client, ADR-0014). Heavy C++ build (rocksdb).

## Scope
fedimint client integration (existing federation + gateway) on fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb; gateway-issued invoice carrying external_id (idempotent on external_id, §6.6); ecash-received settlement stream -> Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat}; ecash-out refund via pay() (idempotent on idempotency_key); satisfies the §6.1 PaymentBackend trait stubbed in backends.rs (FedimintPayment becomes the primary impl; the M0 stub `bail!`s are replaced; phoenixd is M3).

## Non-goals
phoenixd + LND backends (M3); running a guardian (the operator JOINS an existing federation); a custom sqlite IRawDatabase (deferred, ADR-0015); musl-static packaging (M4 at most).

## Constraints / failure handling
settlement = ecash received for the requested invoice; the client tracks which invoice -> which order (external_id, UNIQUE); refunds pay out via the gateway to the buyer's BOLT12 offer / LN address, idempotent on the durable key (no double-refund, §6.6); gateway/federation unavailability surfaces as a backend error; settlements can be redelivered on client reconnect (capture stays idempotent, §6.6). The fedimint RocksDB dir + federation invite/config must be backed up alongside our sqlite (lnrent-7fp.14, ADR-0004/0015).

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane B). Unblocks: capture (lnrent-7fp.8), refund (lnrent-7fp.11).

## Verification
Integration against a test federation + gateway (or a mock PaymentBackend for the e2e).

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.1, §6.4, §6.6, §12; ADR-0003, ADR-0009, ADR-0012, ADR-0015.

## Acceptance Criteria
- create_invoice(.., external_id) returns a gateway bolt11 (idempotent on external_id — a re-call returns the same invoice); on payment, watch() emits Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat} when ecash is received.
- A redelivered settlement (client reconnect) is replayable so capture stays idempotent.
- pay(dest, amount, key) pays a refund out via the gateway, idempotent on the key (a retry never double-pays); payment_status_by_key(key) returns the OUTBOUND `PayStatus`.
- The §6.1 PaymentBackend trait is satisfied by the Fedimint impl (fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb, ADR-0015); the daemon builds glibc-dynamic; the e2e (lnrent-7fp.15) runs it against a mock/test federation.

## M1a DECISION (2026-06-24): mock first
The operator chose a MOCK over a live federation for the M1a money path. Delivered: **MockPayment** (daemon/src/backends.rs) — deterministic in-memory PaymentBackend implementing the full §6.1 trait (create_invoice idempotent on external_id, pay idempotent on key, lookup with injectable-clock absolute expiry, watch()/settle()), + 5 tests. capture(.8) is built+proven on it. **This bead (the REAL fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb backend) is DEFERRED** — it is no longer on the M1a money-path critical path; it lands when wiring a real federation (target TBD). The trait + Settlement model already exist.
close_reason: Fedimint PaymentBackend DONE + proven live. fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb behind a 'fedimint' feature; FedimintPayment satisfies the full async PaymentBackend trait (create_invoice idempotent-on-external_id via an lnrent-owned sqlite index + gateway bolt11 + extra_meta; watch() live/recovery settlement fan-in; lookup; pay() idempotent-on-key via gateway pay_bolt11_invoice, Lightning+Internal; payment_status[_by_key]; oplog crash-window backfill). Commits f20f578(.4a async trait)/5344269(.4b)/d4d3657(.4c.2 receive)/7486dcd(.4c.3 pay)/3c6acee(codex P1/P2 fixes)/a9110fc(.4d). PROVEN GREEN against a real fedimint 0.11.1 devimint regtest (daemon/tests/fedimint_live.rs): received 1000 sat ecash + paid 500 sat out, idempotent. main.rs still rejects payment_backend=fedimint — production enablement for real buyers is gated on a separate LNURL/BOLT12 refund-dest resolver bead (refund_dest is BOLT12/LN-addr, pay()=bolt11) + the main.rs wiring.

title: Nostr engine: NIP-99 listings + NIP-17 DM protocol
description:
## Outcome
A Nostr engine that (a) publishes NIP-99 classified listings (kind 30402, with an operator tag AND the recipe's declared management operations) for the operator's offers, and (b) speaks the lnrent DM protocol over NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs: order.request, order.invoice, order.error, provision.ready, delivery.resend.request, billing.invoice, billing.notice, billing.refund, renew.request, sub.cancel. Buyer->operator request DMs (order.request, renew.request) carry a client `id`; the operator's responses carry the matching `request_id` (correlation + idempotency, §5.1). (The op.request/op.result DISPATCH is the operator op-dispatch bead lnrent-7fp.20, built on this engine's inbound-DM + reply primitives.)

## Why
The marketplace is Nostr-native (NIP-99 discovery + NIP-17 transaction); NIP-90 was rejected as ill-fitting. NIP-17 over public relays has no ordering/ack/delivery guarantee, so the protocol needs message IDs, idempotency, and buyer resync (codex/outside-voice P1).

## Scope
rust-nostr (nostr-sdk) integration; publish/replace 30402 listings (carrying the operator tag + the recipe's `[[operation]]` declarations, so buyers can discover the ops surface before ordering, §7.4); gift-wrap (NIP-59/44) encode/decode of the DM JSON; per-message id + idempotent handling of duplicates; publish/subscribe across several default relays; account-0 key signing (single key in M1a).

## Non-goals
Operator manifest / per-box key verification (M5); the web buyer shell (lnrent-7fp.18, also M1a). The durable outbox SENDER lives in the provision+outbox bead (lnrent-7fp.10); this bead provides the publish/subscribe primitives it uses. The op.request -> op.result DISPATCH (auth + run hook + reply) is lnrent-7fp.20; this bead only provides the inbound-DM + reply primitives it consumes.

## Constraints / failure handling
Relays drop events: publish to multiple relays, dedupe inbound by message id, support buyer resync (delivery.resend.request prompts provision.ready redelivery; renew.request is renewal-invoice only). Do not trust gift-wrap created_at for expiry (use payload fields).

## Dependencies
Depends on: the wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19) — built ON it, does not own it. Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10), operator op-dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20). (The CLI buyer lnrent-7fp.13 depends on the codec lnrent-7fp.19, NOT on this operator engine.)

## Verification
Integration over a local relay.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.2, §5.3, §5.4 (publishes op declarations in the 30402 listing), §7.4; eng-review T5; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A NIP-99 (30402) listing publishes and is fetchable from a local relay, carrying the operator tag AND the recipe's declared management operations.
- order.request -> order.invoice -> provision.ready round-trips over NIP-17 on a local relay.
- A duplicate inbound DM (same message id) is processed exactly once.
- A dropped order.invoice is recovered by the buyer re-sending order.request (idempotent by order/request id); renew.request covers only renewal resync.

## Note (codex)
This bead owns the NIP-17/NIP-99 TRANSPORT primitives (gift-wrap encode/decode, publish/subscribe across relays, message-id dedupe). The order/invoice business orchestration is owned by lnrent-7fp.17; the op.request/op.result management dispatch is owned by lnrent-7fp.20.

## Boundary (polish)
The wire codec (DM message types + NIP-17 gift-wrap + 30402 schema) is the shared lib lnrent-7fp.19. This bead is the OPERATOR-side engine — publish listings, handle inbound order + op DMs (decode/dedupe at the transport level, then hand order DMs to lnrent-7fp.17 and op DMs to lnrent-7fp.20), the outbox publish primitive, multi-relay publish/subscribe — built ON that codec with an injected signer (operator key from lnrent-7fp.16). It no longer owns the codec.
close_reason: Operator Nostr transport engine implemented (daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs, ~1300 lines): multi-relay publish/subscribe, 30402 listing publish (operator tag + op declarations), inbound kind-1059 gift-wrap decode via lnrent-wire + outer-event-id dedup (seen_message migration 2), injected OrderHandler/OpHandler/Outbound seams, paged lag/restart backfill, semaphore DoS backpressure. Built via rb-lite (10-round claude,codex loop) + a codex-found P1 (unpaged backfill) fix round; final codex xhigh GO. 9 local-relay integration tests; 105 workspace tests green. Commits 5b40c7a/3be67d1.

title: Recipe runner + trivial M1a recipe
description:
## Outcome
A hook runner that executes a recipe's lifecycle hooks (provision/suspend/resume/destroy/healthcheck) AND its buyer-facing management-operation hooks (`ops/<hook>`, §7.4) as child processes with: a timeout (kill on expiry), bounded stdout/stderr capture, JSON on stdin (subscription+instance+validated params+host facts), JSON result on stdout, run OFF the reconcile loop. Plus ONE trivial instant recipe for M1a (a WireGuard peer or a dummy 'echo creds' service) that also declares a minimal request-kind management op (`status` / `restart`) so the handshake AND the ops interface have something to exercise.

## Why
A hung/runaway provision must not wedge the reconcile loop (codex/eng-review). Recipes are trusted code (ADR-0002) but still fallible. M1a needs a fast, reliable recipe to prove the loop without VM complexity. Management ops (ADR-0013) run on the same hook-runner machinery, so the runner owns both.

## Scope
process spawn with timeout + output cap + JSON stdin/stdout (shared by lifecycle hooks and management-op hooks); Recipe::validate() (hooks exist+executable, backend known, os supported, AND `[[operation]]` declarations valid — kind in {request, interactive}; `hook` is a SAFE BARE NAME (no path separators, no `..`, no absolute) resolved as `ops/<hook>` — `Operation::hook_is_safe` (already in recipe.rs); for request-kind the resolved `ops/<hook>` exists+executable; op names unique; AND, as defense-in-depth, the canonicalized resolved path stays inside the recipe's `ops/` dir so a stray symlink cannot escape — recipes are trusted (ADR-0002) but this catches author mistakes); the trivial recipe dir + lifecycle hooks + a minimal `ops/` hook.

## Non-goals
Incus/VM recipe (M1b); third-party recipe install (never, ADR-0002). The operator-side op DISPATCH (inbound op.request -> auth -> run hook -> op.result) is lnrent-7fp.20; this bead only provides the runner that executes an op hook and the validation of operation declarations.

## Constraints / failure handling
timeout -> kill + treat as provision/op failure -> refund path (provision) or op.result error (op); secrets via stdin JSON, not argv/env; idempotent lifecycle hooks (re-run safe) for crash recovery (management-op hooks are not assumed idempotent — that is handled at dispatch, lnrent-7fp.20).

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane E). Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10), operator op dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20).

## Verification
Unit + a smoke provision + a smoke management-op run.

## Spec refs
SPEC §7, §7.2, §7.4, §8.1; ADR-0002, ADR-0013; eng-review T8, T10.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A hook exceeding its timeout is killed and surfaces as failure (unit), for both lifecycle and management-op hooks.
- Oversized stdout/stderr is truncated to the cap, not unbounded.
- Nonzero exit surfaces as failure; Recipe::validate() rejects a recipe with a missing/non-executable hook, unknown backend, or unsupported OS.
- Recipe::validate() rejects an `[[operation]]` with an unknown `kind`, a non-bare/traversal `hook` (e.g. `../x`, `a/b`, absolute), a duplicate op name, or (for request-kind) a missing/non-executable `ops/<hook>`; it also rejects a hook whose canonicalized path escapes the recipe `ops/` dir (symlink-escape test).
- The trivial recipe provisions and returns a delivery-payload JSON on stdout, AND its minimal request-kind op hook runs and returns JSON on stdout.

## Tier validation (codex)
- Recipe::validate() rejects a missing or invalid security tier (one of 0/1/1.5/2); the Listing carries an honest tier (ADR-0007, §9.1).

title: Pre-flight + capacity reservation
description:
## Outcome
Order-time capacity reservation plus order-param validation. At pre-flight (before the invoice is issued) the daemon validates the order's params against the recipe [[params]], checks available >= requested, and atomically (via the store actor) creates a reservation HELD for the order with TTL = invoice expiry. available = host budget - (active Instances + live reservations). Reservation released on expiry/refund, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

## Why
Pre-flight without reservation lets two concurrent orders race the last slot into capture-then-refund — on a one-box host the scarce resource (RAM/disk/ports) is the second sale, not an edge case (outside-voice P1). Params must be validated before money moves.

## Scope
operator-configured host budget; param validation against the recipe manifest; the reservation table lifecycle (HELD/CONSUMED/RELEASED); pre-flight check + reserve in one store transaction.

## Non-goals
Runtime per-Instance cgroup limits (the compute backend enforces those in M1b).

## Constraints / failure handling
reserve is atomic with the availability check (no TOCTOU); reservation stays HELD across the whole PROVISIONING window so a concurrent order can't reuse the slot mid-provision.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Unit (concurrent reservation) + integration.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.4, §9.3, §7.1; eng-review T9; outside-voice/codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two concurrent orders for the last slot: exactly one reservation is HELD, the other rejected before any invoice (no TOCTOU).
- Order params are validated against the recipe [[params]] at pre-flight; invalid params reject before invoice.
- Reservation is RELEASED on invoice expiry, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, and becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

title: Idempotent settlement capture
description:
## Outcome
Idempotent, **invoice-status-first** handling of ALL incoming settlements. On a Settlement, look up the invoice by external_id (UNIQUE, NOT NULL), then in ONE transaction `UPDATE invoice SET status='PAID', settled_at=?, applied_at=? WHERE id=? AND status='OPEN'` together with the resulting subscription move, decided by the SETTLED INVOICE, not the sub state alone:
- already-PAID/applied invoice -> **no-op** (backend redelivery / replay);
- OPEN **order** invoice (sub PENDING) -> PROVISIONING (the capture);
- OPEN **renewal** invoice (sub ACTIVE/SUSPENDED) -> extend/resume, setting `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period`;
- **EXPIRED / terminal / unmatched** invoice -> exactly **one** refund_attempt (settled-but-terminal), keyed by a deterministic refund key (`refund:<external_id>`, §6.6) so a redelivered settlement cannot create a second refund.
A replayed settlement affects 0 rows (status guard) and is a no-op, so paid_through can never double-extend.

## Why
the payment backend can redeliver a settlement (e.g. a Fedimint client / phoenixd ws reconnect); the event_log alone prevents double-running a hook, not double-counting a payment. The idempotency guard is the invoice status (OPEN->PAID CAS), and the outcome is chosen by invoice class — NOT by subscription state alone, so a renewal settlement in ACTIVE is never mis-routed to a refund (codex #3/#5; convergence fix). Renewal and first payment share the same settlement machinery.

## Scope
settlement -> invoice (external_id) -> subscription mapping; the atomic status-guard transaction for first capture, renewal extension (max formula), resume-on-late-renewal, and settled-but-terminal refund routing with a deterministic refund key.

## Non-goals
Running provision (lnrent-7fp.10); refund EXECUTION (lnrent-7fp.11); deadline firing (lnrent-7fp.9 reconcile).

## Constraints / failure handling
external_id UNIQUE + NOT NULL per invoice; one transaction per settlement; already-PAID invoice -> no-op; EXPIRED or terminal-sub settlement -> exactly one refund_attempt (deterministic refund key prevents duplicates on redelivery), not provisioning.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1), the payment backend (Fedimint, lnrent-7fp.4 — its `watch()` settlement stream). Unblocks: provision+outbox, refund, e2e.

## Verification
Unit + integration with duplicate and renewal settlements.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.6; ADR-0009; eng-review T3; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A duplicate Settlement for the same invoice extends paid_through exactly once (idempotent; unit + integration).
- A first-invoice settlement moves PENDING -> PROVISIONING; a renewal settlement sets `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period`; a renewal while SUSPENDED (in retention) resumes + extends.
- A Settlement for an EXPIRED invoice OR on a terminal subscription produces exactly ONE refund_attempt — enforced by `UNIQUE(refund_attempt.idempotency_key)` (`refund:<external_id>`) + `INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` in the settlement txn, so a redelivered settlement reuses the one row, never a second — and never a provision.
- UNIQUE(external_id) holds: a second insert with the same external_id fails.

## Settled-but-terminal ownership (codex)
This bead only DETECTS a settlement on an EXPIRED/terminal invoice and ROUTES it: it creates a **detached** refund_attempt (PENDING, deterministic key) and leaves the subscription terminal (it does NOT enter REFUND_DUE — that state is reserved for a captured order whose provision failed, lnrent-7fp.10/.11). The refund EXECUTION (pay/retry/status/alert) is owned by lnrent-7fp.11.
close_reason: Capture implemented (daemon/src/capture.rs): invoice-status-first idempotent settlement state machine + 11 tests, built on the PaymentBackend trait + MockPayment (mock chosen for M1a; real Fedimint .4 deferred). Codex xhigh GO: sound base for .11/.10. Dropped the stale .8->.4 edge (capture needs the trait, which exists, not the real backend).

title: Reconcile loop + total state machine (core deadline transitions)
description:
## Outcome
A single periodic reconcile loop that advances subscriptions by their DEADLINES: scan rows whose
`next_deadline <= now` and fire the due transition, recomputing `next_deadline`. Implements the
TOTAL SubState machine (§6.3) via a compare-and-swap UPDATE on `(state, next_deadline)` — the
subscription row IS the guard. **Downtime credit (heartbeat + outage-window deadline shift) is SPLIT
OUT to its own bead (depends on this) — keep this bead to the core tick + deadline transitions.**

## Scope (the core deadline transitions + the CAS guard + totality — nothing more)
- **soft_date reminder** (ACTIVE, now >= soft_date, not yet reminded): issue the soft-date renewal
  invoice (REUSE order_intake renewal issuance, deterministic `renew:auto:<sub>:<paid_through>`
  external_id) and ENQUEUE billing.invoice + billing.notice to the `outbox` (.10's sender delivers —
  no direct publish). Advance next_deadline to paid_through.
- **suspend** (ACTIVE, now >= paid_through): ACTIVE -> SUSPENDED; fire the recipe `suspend` hook
  (runner .6, idempotent); enqueue billing.notice. next_deadline = paid_through + retention_s.
- **destroy / terminate** (SUSPENDED or CANCELLED, now >= retention end): -> TERMINATED; fire the
  recipe `destroy` hook (best-effort); RELEASE the reservation (reservation::release_txn, .7);
  next_deadline cleared. TERMINATED is the single finalized terminal both expiry and cancel reach.
- **PENDING order expiry** (now >= next_deadline = the order invoice expiry set by .17): sub ->
  EXPIRED, OPEN order invoice -> EXPIRED.
- **renewal invoice expiring unpaid**: a no-op on SUBSCRIPTION state — only that invoice -> EXPIRED.

## Totality
Any NON-settlement `(state, event)` with no defined transition above is a LOGGED no-op (not a
panic/error). Do NOT implement settlement handling — invoice-status-first, owned by capture (.8).
The renewal `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period` formula is CAPTURE's, applied on
SETTLEMENT; reconcile fires only the soft_date REMINDER (it does NOT extend paid_through itself).

## Constraints / failure handling
Absolute wall-clock deadlines; each transition is a CAS on `(state, next_deadline)` so a
replayed/stale/concurrent fire affects 0 rows (no double-run); idempotent + journaled to event_log.
Buyer DMs are ENQUEUED to the outbox, never published directly. Side effects: suspend/destroy hooks
via runner (.6); reservation release on TERMINATED (.7); soft-date renewal issuance via order-intake
(.17). KEEP IT BOUNDED: do NOT modify capture/order_intake/ipc/store beyond adding a thin
txn-composable helper if strictly required; the machine is finite (9 states) — no speculative
states/events/abstractions/config/scheduling framework (a `next_deadline <= now` scan IS the
scheduler).

## Non-goals
Downtime credit (split bead); settlement/capture (.8); refund execution + settled-but-terminal
auto-refund (.8/.11); provision/deliver (.10 — reconcile only ENQUEUES); the daemon supervisor that
calls the tick on an interval (.21 — expose `reconcile_tick(now)` it drives).

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (.1), clock (.2), recipe runner (.6), reservation (.7), order-intake issuance
(.17). Unblocks: refund (.11), e2e (.15).

## Acceptance Criteria (unit, TestClock)
- Each transition fires at its deadline: soft_date -> reminder + renewal invoice enqueued + cursor
  advanced; paid_through -> SUSPENDED + suspend hook + billing.notice enqueued; retention end ->
  TERMINATED + destroy hook + reservation RELEASED; PENDING order-expiry -> EXPIRED.
- A renewal invoice expiring unpaid changes ONLY the invoice (-> EXPIRED), not the sub state.
- CANCELLED -> TERMINATED after the retention destroy completes.
- A transition is a CAS on `(state, next_deadline)`: a replayed/stale fire affects 0 rows. A
  NON-settlement `(state, event)` with no defined transition is a logged no-op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.5; ADR-0005; eng-review T6; codex (total state machine).
close_reason: Core reconcile loop + finite 9-state deadline transitions + CAS guard implemented (daemon/src/reconcile.rs, ~800 impl lines): reconcile_tick scans next_deadline<=now, fires the due transition as a CAS on (state,next_deadline) (replay=0 rows), journaled, buyer DMs enqueued to outbox (.10 delivers): PENDING order expiry; soft_date reminder (self-contained renew:auto invoice, expiry sized to the renewal window) + billing DMs; suspend+suspend-hook; destroy+destroy-hook+reservation release_txn; renewal-invoice-unpaid expiry; totality=logged no-op. Renewal-side transitions guard against backend-paid-but-capture-pending invoices (renewal_settlement_pending mirrors order_invoice_may_expire). Built via HARD-BOUNDED rb-lite (--max-rounds 6, --min-severity P1, file-locked) after an un-bounded attempt blew up + was discarded + the bead re-scoped (downtime credit split to .22). Manually fixed 2 codex P1s (renewal-invoice expiry sizing; backend-paid-renewal defer); final codex xhigh GO. 169 tests green. Deferred: single-shot reminders (repeated = post-M1a). Commits on master.

title: lnv2 full-daemon staging acceptance: buyer golden path + refund arm
description:
Full-daemon staging dogfood of the REFUND path on the LNV2 backend (retargeted 2026-07-17 per ADR-0018 — do NOT run this on lnv1, which is scheduled for deletion in lnrent-8ym; passing this dogfood IS the trigger for that deletion).

o6p's code is complete + happy-path proven live (real buyer -> ecash -> DO droplet -> SSH -> cancel, + the 3-seller/5-buyer marketplace sim) — on lnv1. NOT yet exercised live: the REFUND leg through the full daemon, now on lnv2. Run lnrentd with payment_backend=fedimint (which selects the LNV2 impl once lnrent-3d5 lands) + the REAL refund Resolver (ug8 — backend-agnostic) against a signet/mutinynet/devimint federation WITH THE LNV2 MODULE ENABLED (released fedimintd v0.11.x supports it; the devimint lnv2 harness is the reference for spinning it), and drive: (a) a late-order / failed-provision REFUND_DUE -> refund paid out; (b) an LN-address AND an LNURL refund_dest resolved to a fresh bolt11 at refund time (proves the Resolver end-to-end on lnv2). Mainnet enablement itself stays the OPERATOR's action per docs/go-live.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Staging federation runs the lnv2 module; daemon uses the lnv2 backend (lnrent-3d5).
- (a) late-order / failed-provision REFUND_DUE pays out a refund; (b) BOTH an LN-address AND an LNURL refund_dest resolve to a fresh bolt11 at refund time.
- Evidence captured: daemon logs + ledger showing each refund_attempt SENT and the buyer-side receipt for (a) and (b); the lnv2 final-state (FinalSendOperationState) observed for each payout.
- Doctor/preflight passes its lnv2 functional probe against the staging federation before the run.
- Outcome: green end-to-end => this bead closes AND lnrent-8ym (delete lnv1) unblocks; any failure files follow-up beads rather than closing.
## Expanded scope (second-model audit B2/N2 — this run authorizes lnv1 deletion, so it must prove the FULL product on lnv2, not just refunds)
- FULL BUYER GOLDEN PATH on lnv2: discover -> order -> pay (real wallet) -> lnv2 settlement -> capture -> provision (real droplet) -> credentials over DM -> cancel -> teardown. The prior golden-path proof was on lnv1 and does not transfer.
- RESTART RECOVERY: daemon restarted (a) during an inbound settlement window and (b) during a refund in flight — both recover via lnv2 final states, no lost/duplicated money.
- OPERATOR SWEEP: one real sweep with a fresh invoice on lnv2.
- REFUND CASES clarified: two distinct refund triggers (late-order AND failed-provision), and across them BOTH resolver types exercised (LN-address once, LNURL once minimum); evidence maps one-to-one to each case.
- Evidence for every leg: daemon logs, ledger rows, lnv2 FinalSend/FinalReceive states, buyer receipts, provisioning artifacts.
close_reason: lnv2 full-daemon acceptance PASSED live on mainnet solomint + real DO: golden path (incl. teardown), failed-provision refund x2 with LNURL resolver verified against recurringd, restart recovery in both windows (refund recovered to exactly one attempt), operator sweep with buyer receipt. LN-address form covered by full-resolve tests; late-order trigger is black-box unreachable and unit-test covered. Authorizes lnrent-8ym.

comments.author: master
comments.text: RETARGETED (2026-07-17, ADR-0018 + grill Q6): the dogfood runs on the LNV2 backend (lnrent-3d5), not lnv1 — do not spend the acceptance run on scaffolding scheduled for deletion (lnrent-8ym). Staging federation must be spun with the lnv2 module ENABLED (released fedimintd v0.11.x supports it; devimint lnv2 harness as reference). Passing this dogfood is the trigger for lnv1 deletion.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
PROBE VALIDATED against a live lnv2 federation (2026-07-18). Used the solomint invite at /tmp/solomint-invite.txt — single-guardian federation at wss://api.solo.f.blindguardian.net/ (fed id 04e550da…), 7 modules INCLUDING lnv2 (also mintv2/walletv2). Verified end-to-end, MONEY-FREE:
- our pinned v0.11.1 fedimint client joins cleanly; guardians reachable (session_count=569, advancing).
- daemon boots payment_backend=fedimint and logs "lnv2 fedimint payment backend joined; real ecash money path active".
- `lnrent preflight` (alias doctor) runs the lnv2 functional probe correctly: federation=PASS (guardians reachable), lnv2=FAIL "lnv2 module present but no lnv2 gateway is attached to the federation" (Lnv2Probe::GatewayAbsent), gateway=FAIL "no lnv2 gateway is registered on the federation", provider_token=SKIP.

SOLE BLOCKER for the money legs: no lnv2 gateway is vetted into the federation. `fedimint-cli module lnv2 gateways list` = [] and `select` = "No gateways are available". A gateway (fenix-ostracoda, https://ostracoda.gw.blindguardian.net/v1, id 022dba77…) IS announced but only via the lnv1 `ln` module (vetted:false there); the lnv2 vetted list is empty.

NEXT (operator/guardian action, needs real Lightning liquidity): stand up / confirm an lnv2-capable Lightning gateway and VET it into the federation's lnv2 module (guardian action). Once the probe flips to lnv2=Healthy, drive the full dogfood: buyer golden path + refund arm via daemon/tests/lnv2_live.rs (FM_INVITE_CODE env) + a live daemon run. The read-only probe + federation-join half of 7qc acceptance is now proven; the money legs remain.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
GATEWAY VETTED INTO lnv2 — probe now PASSES (2026-07-18). The ostracoda gateway (fenix-ostracoda) is now vetted into the solomint's lnv2 module, so the daemon's lnv2 functional probe flips GatewayAbsent -> Healthy.

Root cause of the earlier GatewayAbsent: lnv2 has NO auto-registration for the vetted-gateway set (unlike lnv1's TTL auto-register). `list_gateways` reads the federation's vetted set (GATEWAYS_ENDPOINT); adding to it is a GUARDIAN admin op — `fedimint-cli module lnv2 gateways add <url>` -> `module_api.add_gateway(auth, SafeUrl)` -> request_admin(ADD_GATEWAY_ENDPOINT, auth). The gateway was serving lnv1 (auto-registered) but never vetted for lnv2.

What I did: confirmed the ostracoda gateway fully supports lnv2 (POST https://ostracoda.gw.blindguardian.net/v1/routing_info -> 200 RoutingInfo: LN pubkey 02dfecc8…, receive_fee 2000/5000ppm, send_fee 4000/10000ppm). Then vetted it as the solo guardian (peer id 0, password from the guardian pod /var/lib/fedimint/password.private): `fedimint-cli --our-id 0 --password <pw> module lnv2 gateways add https://ostracoda.gw.blindguardian.net/v1` -> returned true. `module lnv2 gateways list`/`select` now return that URL.

Daemon `lnrent preflight` (doctor) now: gateway=PASS "refund gateway reachable", federation=PASS, lnv2=PASS "lnv2 module present and an lnv2 gateway is reachable" (Lnv2Probe::Healthy), provider_token=SKIP -> Preflight: PASS.

7qc acceptance now unblocked on infra: the "Doctor/preflight passes its lnv2 functional probe against the staging federation before the run" criterion is MET. The federation is GO for the money-path dogfood.

Remaining for 7qc money legs (need a real buyer wallet + DO_TOKEN provisioning, no longer infra-blocked): buyer golden path (order->pay real wallet->lnv2 settlement->capture->provision droplet->creds DM->cancel->teardown); refund arm (late-order AND failed-provision, LN-address + LNURL resolver each once); restart recovery (settlement window + refund in flight); one operator sweep. Drive via daemon/tests/lnv2_live.rs (FM_INVITE_CODE=<solomint invite>) + a live daemon run. Gateway solomint balance ~98,994 sat available; the gateway also serves 2 OTHER mainnet feds (E-Cash Club, School of Sats) — do not disturb.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LIVE MONEY ATTEMPT — buyer/invoice-payability BLOCKER found (2026-07-18). The gateway vetting works (probe=Healthy), but paying the solomint's lnv2 receive invoice from the available fedimint-cli wallets does NOT work. Established with real (tiny, refundable) mainnet sats:

RAW fedimint proof (no lnrent code yet): `fedimint-cli --data-dir <solomint> module lnv2 receive "10 sat"` mints a bolt11 whose incoming contract is 7950 msat (2050 msat lnv2 receive fee — CONFIRMS the net-of-fee premise). Paying it:
- SCHOOL of Sats (lnv1, served BY ostracoda): `module ln pay` selected ostracoda -> ostracoda must pay its OWN lnv2 invoice = self-loop; gateway never funds the lnv2 incoming contract; receive stays Pending, pay stays pending (~12 sat locked, will refund on contract timeout). Gateways here do NOT do lnv1-out -> lnv2-in internal swaps.
- MARIGOLD (lnv1 pay via a FOREIGN gateway 'Fedi us-east-1'): gateway returns "Lightning Gateway failed to pay invoice ... Invalid request", route_hints:[] , destination is an lnv2 blinded-path key (not ostracoda's node 02dfecc8). A plain lnv1 gateway can't pay an lnv2 (route-blinded) invoice. (~22 sat locked, will refund.)
- lnv2->lnv2 path unavailable: the lnv2-capable wallets (orange/thebaby/bitcrazy/libertytree/kibera/marigold/manifold/harlem) all have EMPTY lnv2 vetted-gateway lists (same state the solomint was in before I vetted ostracoda), so none can `module lnv2 send`.

ROOT CAUSE (fedimint interop, NOT an lnrent bug): a fedimint lnv2 receive invoice can only be settled by (a) ostracoda internally from an lnv2 payer in a fed ostracoda serves (only Solomint qualifies -> needs solomint ecash, circular), or (b) an external payer that supports paying lnv2/blinded invoices over real LN to fenix-ostracoda (a modern LN wallet / Fedi app / an lnv2 gateway). Common lnv1 fedimint gateways reject it. This is a real BUYER-COMPATIBILITY finding for launch/docs.

NEEDED to finish 7qc money legs: an lnv2-capable payer -> an external LN wallet (Fedi app / Phoenix / LND with blinded-path support) paying the operator's bolt11, OR a funded solomint client paying via lnv2 send internally. Stuck test sats (~34) refund automatically. lnrent's Lnv2Payment code is NOT implicated — the block is upstream invoice payability in this environment.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CORRECTION + deeper diagnosis (2026-07-19). My earlier comment wrongly said the lnv2 invoice is "route-blinded" and that's why lnv1 gateways can't pay it. That was WRONG — retracted. Verified via fenix `lncli decodepayreq`: the invoice is a plain bolt11 to fenix-ostracoda (02dfecc8), route_hints:[], features tlv-onion+payment-addr (required), MPP+route-blinding (OPTIONAL, is_required=false, no blinded path present). No blinding, no invoice defect.

WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENS (raw fedimint-cli reconstruction, NOT lnrent code):
- The lnv2 receive path WORKS: unrelated parallel ~1000-sat traffic on this gateway confirmed in consensus during testing (gateway logs: "Transaction accepted in consensus").
- My 15-sat attempt via marigold->Henwen gateway REACHED fenix (gateway logs: LND HODL invoice payment_hash=6832a30e state=3 = HTLC ACCEPTED/held) and ostracoda intercepted + submitted the funding tx for the operator contract (12925 msat net). But it did NOT complete: my "operator" was a one-shot `fedimint-cli` client, not a persistent receiver. lnv2 receive REQUIRES the receiver online to CLAIM the funded contract (revealing the preimage that settles the payer's held HTLC). My one-shot client + slow mainnet walletv2 scans + me killing/restarting await-receive meant the claim never reliably ran, so the HODL HTLC stayed held and the loop never closed.
- School (lnv1 via ostracoda-its-own-gateway) = self-payment (payee == the paying gateway's node), can't route. Fedi/Henwen gateways returned "Invalid request" on the client's retries, but Henwen still pushed the HTLC to fenix (state=3) before the receiver-claim gap stalled it.

CONCLUSION: NO bug in lnrent (never ran) or fedimint (path works). The blocker was my fragile hand-rolled test harness (no persistent online claimer). The RIGHT way to prove 7qc's receive leg is the actual lnrent daemon's `Lnv2Payment` with its persistent `watch()` receive task (or `daemon/tests/lnv2_live.rs`), which stays online to claim — not manual fedimint-cli on both ends. Buyer must be a proper LN payer whose gateway isn't ostracoda (Henwen reaches fenix fine).

MONEY: ~51 test sats locked in pending outgoing contracts (school ~12, marigold ~39 across two attempts); all refund on contract timeout. Nothing lost.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LIVE RECEIVE BLOCKER = solomint infra, NOT lnrent (2026-07-19). After funding a proper buyer and using a persistent claimer, the operator-side lnv2 receive on THIS solomint still won't complete:
- Buyer path WORKS: funded marigold to 66.7k msat via school->fedimint.org-gw->Henwen->marigold ({"status":"paid"}); real LN payments reach fenix (HODL invoice state=3 confirmed for every attempt).
- Gateway funds the lnv2 IncomingContract on the solomint (submits tx, e.g. 8e26abc5 op 34bc5080, 17900 msat net) but it NEVER reaches "accepted in consensus" — silently stalls (no accept, no reject, no error). No gateway tx confirmed to fed 7dd868a8 in a 15-min window.
- The fedimint-cli operator client HANGS on the solomint's walletv2 "Scanning for outputs" — even `info` and `dev list-operation-states` time out (progressively worse), so balance/claim can't complete.
- The solomint GUARDIAN is healthy: block sync current (processed 958637, tip 958638), sessions completing ~every 61s.

KEY: lnrent's lnv2 backend attaches only lnv2+mint(v1)+wallet(v1), NOT walletv2 (lnv2_backend.rs:1843) — so the OPERATOR side would avoid this hang with the real daemon. But the GATEWAY's funding tx to the solomint not confirming blocks ANY receiver. The solomint was deployed with all v2 modules (ln,lnv2,meta,mint,mintv2,wallet,walletv2); mintv2+walletv2 are EXTRA (lnrent never uses them) and walletv2 is the prime suspect for the client stalls.

NOT a bug in lnrent (never ran) or the fedimint protocol (parallel ~1000-sat traffic + marigold's own receive both confirmed fine on other feds). RECOMMENDATION: redeploy the solomint LEANER (lnv2 + v1 mint/wallet only; leave FM_ENABLE_MODULE_MINTV2/WALLETV2 OFF — the solomint-skill DEFAULT), which matches exactly what lnrent uses and should avoid the walletv2 client hang; then re-vet the gateway and retry. Alternatively investigate the gateway's solomint client (possibly stuck on walletv2; a gateway restart would clear it but risks the other mainnet feds it serves). MONEY: test sats held/pending across attempts, all refundable; funding marigold from school was a wash between our own wallets.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE LOCALIZED (2026-07-19) — a fedimint GATEWAY/GUARDIAN issue, NOT lnrent, NOT blinding.

Reproducible finding (5 lnv2 intercepts over 2h on the solomint, only 1 confirmed):
- The gateway funds the operator's lnv2 IncomingContract by submitting a fedimint tx to the solomint guardian (`handle_lightning_payment` -> `fm::client::net::api: Finalized and submitting transaction`).
- For the ONE that CONFIRMED (a ~1000-sat receive): 8 EXACT mint-note inputs, 1 output (the lnv2 contract), NO mint change. The submission SM logged `accepted by peer` -> `accepted in consensus`. Money landed.
- For all 4 that STALLED (my 15-20 sat receives): 1 mint-note input, 1 lnv2 contract output, + ~12 MINT CHANGE outputs. The gateway logs ONLY `Finalized and submitting transaction` and then NOTHING — the `submit_transaction` API call to the guardian never returns, the submission SM never advances, no error, no rejection. The HTLC stays held at fenix (HODL state=3); the operator never sees a funded contract; the buyer retries "Gateway Internal Error / Invalid request".

=> Trigger: an lnv2 receive whose gateway-funding tx must carry MINT CHANGE outputs hangs at guardian `submit_transaction`. The exact-note/no-change case confirms fine. This affects most SMALL receives (gateway lacks an exact note, so it makes change).

Ruled out: lnrent code (never ran — raw fedimint-cli both ends), route-blinding (optional, unused; invoice is a plain bolt11 to fenix 02dfecc8 per `lncli decodepayreq`), mint denominations (all tiers 1..2^57 present), max_notes_per_denomination (=3, my txs hit exactly 3, not over; not a hard tx rule anyway), tx balance (balances with fees), nginx ingress timeouts (600s >> 61s session), guardian health (syncs mainnet blocks live, sessions every 61s, accepts other feds' txs; gateway solomint balance dropped ~5M msat over a day from real traffic). Client "hangs" were partly (a) correct await on an unfunded contract and (b) my own leftover retrying fedimint-cli procs holding the client.db lock.

BLOCKED on exact guardian-side reason: the official `fedimint/fedimintd:v0.11.1` image is compiled with DEBUG stripped (`release_max_level_info`) — RUST_LOG=debug yields zero debug lines — so the guardian won't log WHY it doesn't ACK the change-heavy tx. (I bumped the solomint guardian to debug + paused ArgoCD to test this, confirmed stripped, then restored info + re-enabled auto-sync.)

NEXT to pin the exact mechanism: build a DEBUG-enabled fedimintd (v0.11.1 source) image, deploy it to the solomint, reproduce, and capture the guardian's `submit_transaction`/consensus handling of a change-bearing lnv2 tx. Strong candidates: a validation path that mixes an lnv2 Incoming output with mint outputs, or threshold/decryption interaction. This is an UPSTREAM fedimint bug to report + fix; lnrent's lnv2 backend is correct. MONEY: ~5 test sats of actual movement; the rest held in pending/HODL, refundable.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
V2 MODULES ARE NOT BROKEN — local devimint matrix clears them (2026-07-19). Built fedimint at the guardian's EXACT commit 2620789 (all workspace bins, debug-capable, at ~/p/fedimint-dbg/target-nix/debug) and ran controlled lnv2 receive tests on local devimint federations. Each test: vet an LDK gateway into lnv2 (guardian admin add_gateway, password at $FM_DATA_DIR/fedimintd-default-0/password.private), lnv2 receive, pay from LND, await-receive.

RESULTS (large = 1000 sat control; small = 20 sat, the size that hangs on solomint):
- 4 guardians, NO v2 modules       -> large CLAIMED, small CLAIMED
- 4 guardians + mintv2 + walletv2  -> large CLAIMED, small CLAIMED
- 1 guardian  + mintv2 + walletv2  -> large CLAIMED, small CLAIMED (slower; one run exceeded a 90s await timeout but succeeded on retry — SLOW, not broken)

=> lnv2 receive works in EVERY local config, including single-guardian WITH mintv2+walletv2 at small amounts. Therefore: the v2 modules are FINE; "small amount / mint-change outputs" is NOT the cause (my earlier change-output hypothesis is DISPROVEN); single-guardian is slow but functional.

ALSO CORRECTED A MAJOR ERROR: the guardian's debug logs were never actually enabled. ArgoCD `selfHeal` silently reverted every `kubectl set env RUST_LOG=...` — verified by reading RUST_LOG from INSIDE the container (always showed the manifest's `info`). The Application CR is itself GitOps-managed, so patching away `automated` does not stick. Debug is NOT compiled out of fedimintd (no release_max_level anywhere in source; fedimint-logging honors RUST_LOG at runtime). To get guardian debug on the cluster you must edit the GitOps manifest (~/newmachine/remote-devops/k8s/argo/fedimint/solomint.yaml) and let ArgoCD sync; kubectl env edits will always be reverted.

UPSTREAM ISSUE SEARCH: no match found. #8828 ("incoming receive never funded", 2026-07-16) is LNv1-only and explicitly states "LNv2 sidesteps the issue by keying incoming contracts on the payment hash rather than a gateway-local scid". #8812 is an iroh-transport CI flake. #5238 (submit_transaction needs better error handling) explains WHY a failure surfaces as silence rather than an error, but is not the cause.

REMAINING HYPOTHESIS (deployment-specific, not fedimint code): the ostracoda gateway's solomint CLIENT is in a stale/broken state — exactly 1 funding tx confirmed (early, 02:39) and every one since hangs at `submit_transaction` with no response and no error, while the same gateway serves its other federations fine and the solomint guardian is healthy (syncs mainnet blocks, completes sessions, accepted the one tx). A gateway restart would clear that client state and test this directly — but ostracoda also serves E-Cash Club + School of Sats (real mainnet money), so it needs operator sign-off.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (2026-07-19) — guardian REJECTS the gateway's lnv2 funding tx; the gateway SILENTLY SWALLOWS the rejection, which is why it looked like an indefinite hang.

HOW IT WAS FOUND: enabled guardian debug logging the ONLY way that sticks — via the GitOps manifest (commit c2b3ef1 in zalguod/devops, k8s/argo/fedimint/solomint.yaml: RUST_LOG=info,fm=debug,fedimint_server=debug,...), since `kubectl set env` is always reverted by ArgoCD selfHeal. Verified live by reading RUST_LOG INSIDE the container.

THE EVIDENCE (guardian debug log):
  api_req{method="submit_transaction"}: fm::net::api: Transaction rejected
      txid=8e26abc5… err=The transaction had an invalid output: 1-The contract is expired
  (same for e7ba693f, 26e886d6, 730702a6, 0c5571a9 — exactly my failing lnv2 funding txs)

So submissions DO reach the guardian (27 submit_transaction requests logged) and are REJECTED with a specific, actionable error. The gateway's client logs only "Finalized and submitting transaction" and then nothing — no error, no rejection, submission SM never advances. That silent-swallow is upstream issue #5238 ("Replace submit_transaction endpoint with something with better error handling strategy").

WHY "expired": the lnv2 IncomingContract carries a 1-hour expiry (contract expiration 1784432571 = 03:42:51Z for the invoice created 02:43; 1784433863 = 04:04:23Z for the one created 03:04). The captured rejections fired at 15:24 — the gateway replaying its stuck/pending txs after the restart, by which time the contracts were long expired. So the observed rejections are of STALE retries.

RESULTING FAILURE MODE (the real bug): if an lnv2 receive's funding tx does not land within the contract's expiry window, it becomes PERMANENTLY unrecoverable — every retry is rejected "contract is expired", the gateway surfaces no error anywhere, the HTLC stays held at the LN node, and the payer retries forever against a contract that can never be funded. Nothing in the stack reports the failure.

WHAT WAS RULED OUT ALONG THE WAY: lnrent code (never ran — raw fedimint-cli both ends); route-blinding (optional/unused; plain bolt11 to fenix per lncli decodepayreq); mint denominations (all tiers present); max_notes_per_denomination; tx balance; nginx ingress timeouts (600s >> 61s session); guardian health (syncs mainnet blocks, sessions every 61s); and — via a local devimint matrix at the guardian's exact commit 2620789 — the v2 modules and small amounts: lnv2 receive CLAIMED in every local config (4-guardian ±mintv2/walletv2, AND 1-guardian +v2 modules) at both 1000 sat and 20 sat.

GATEWAY RESTART (operator-authorized): ostracoda gatewayd restarted; all FOUR federations reconnected with byte-identical balances (Solomint 93859070, Solomint Iroh 97999358, E-Cash Club 22634181826, School of Sats 6373348607). Post-restart the submission pipeline is demonstrably healthy again — txs now log submitting -> "accepted by peer" -> "accepted in consensus", which had not happened for my txs before.

NEXT: (a) upstream — the gateway must surface submit_transaction rejections instead of swallowing them (#5238), and should not retry a funding tx whose contract has expired (or should re-create the contract); (b) lnrent is unaffected in code but a live operator would see receives silently stall, so this is a launch-relevant environment caveat for lnv2 on this infra. Guardian debug logging is still ON (commit c2b3ef1) — revert that commit when the investigation closes.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CORRECTION (2026-07-19): my earlier "ROOT CAUSE FOUND" comment on this bead OVER-CLAIMED. Retracting the causal conclusion; the observations stand.

WHAT I CLAIMED: that the guardian's "The contract is expired" rejection of the gateway's lnv2 funding tx was the root cause, and (in draft feedback for fedimint PR #8833) that a degraded long-poll could burn the contract's 1h life and make funding permanently impossible.

WHY THAT'S WRONG (verified in modules/fedimint-lnv2-server/src/lib.rs, and independently documented by the PR author who had already made and RETRACTED the same argument):
- The awaiting client is an OBSERVER, not the funder. Funding happens via the gateway's relay_incoming_htlc/relay_direct_swap landing as `submit_transaction`, which is NOT in the long-poll method list (await_incoming_contract / await_incoming_contracts / await_preimage) and so runs on the PROMPT tier — independent of any long-poll connection. A degraded long-poll cannot delay funding.
- CLAIMING has no deadline: `process_input`'s expiration checks exist only in the `LightningInputV0::Outgoing` branch; the `Incoming` branch has NO expiration check, and IncomingContractOutpointKey persists. Once funded, claiming is safe indefinitely.
- The 3600s long-poll budget == 3600s contract expiry match was COINCIDENTAL; the causal chain does not connect.

WHAT REMAINS TRUE:
- Funding-side expiry IS checked: `process_output` for LightningOutputV0::Incoming returns ContractExpired when commitment.expiration_or_fee <= consensus_unix_time. That is the rejection I captured.
- The guardian genuinely rejected 5 funding txids (0c5571a9, e7ba693f, 8e26abc5, 730702a6, 26e886d6) with "The transaction had an invalid output: 1-The contract is expired" — but these were STALE RETRIES replayed hours later (post gateway-restart), by which time the contracts had legitimately expired. They are a downstream consequence, NOT the cause.
- The gateway SWALLOWS submit_transaction rejections (logs only "Finalized and submitting transaction", no error, SM never advances) — upstream #5238. This stands and is the reason an actionable server-side error presented as an indefinite hang.

STILL UNEXPLAINED: why the ORIGINAL funding submissions failed to land. That is the open question; the expired-retry rejections do not answer it.

PROCESS NOTE: I had posted the (wrong) argument as a comment on fedimint PR #8833; it was deleted before any maintainer engaged, and the feedback now lives at /tmp/pr8833-feedback.md marked RETRACTED. Lesson: check whether a PR's own body already addresses an argument before raising it — the author had investigated this more deeply than I had and documented the retraction in the "Open questions" section.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (well-evidenced, 2026-07-19): the gateway retries a stuck lnv2 funding operation by BUILDING A NEW TRANSACTION THAT REUSES THE SAME MINT NOTES, so every retry is rejected as a double-spend — and the rejection is swallowed, so the loop never converges.

Instrumented BOTH sides: guardian at debug (GitOps commit c2b3ef1) and gateway at `fm::client::net::api=trace` (GitOps commit 7f90592).

EVIDENCE
1. Transport is FINE — not a hang. Gateway trace: `Api response method=Core("submit_transaction") res_ok=true` for every submission. The RPC round-trip succeeds; any rejection rides INSIDE that successful response.
2. Guardian rejects a large fraction, with TWO distinct reasons (last 12m: 5 accepted / 5 rejected):
     3 x "The transaction had an invalid input: 3-The note is already spent"   <-- FRESH, the real one
     2 x "The transaction had an invalid output: 1-The contract is expired"    <-- stale tail
3. ONE operation submits MANY DISTINCT txids, reusing the same notes:
     op 7e80b3a1_6d265d9f -> 5 txids (d7a0fb24, c9d61638, a04dc77d, 9e627c70, 06e2193f)
     op 0dfb1e94_a30bfb9b -> 3 txids (df5dfa96, 70c90a5c, 0a29482f) — ALL 3 rejected "note already spent"
     op 50e55ee3_5d14bc08 -> 1 txid (bbc18219)  [my fresh test]
   9 submissions from 3 operations = heavy retry amplification.
4. Same note nonces consumed repeatedly across those txs (guardian "Marking note as spent"):
     0299d6dbabda8d70 x3, 0276458b64399515 x3, 023fe1c56a2a49e1 x3, plus several x2 (23 spend events total).
5. Ops 7e80b3a1 and 0dfb1e94 are MY STUCK INVOICES FROM YESTERDAY — the gateway is still retrying them ~a day later, burning notes each time.
6. Gateway swallows all of it: logs only "Finalized and submitting transaction", no error, submission SM never advances (upstream #5238).

MECHANISM
