  funding op submits tx -> outcome not observed (swallowed) -> op retries by BUILDING A NEW TX with the SAME input notes -> if the first landed, retry rejected "note already spent" -> also swallowed -> retry again ... forever. Meanwhile the 1h contract expires, after which retries additionally fail "contract is expired". Net: the receive never completes, funds/HTLC stay stuck, and NOTHING surfaces an error anywhere.

WHY LOCAL DEVIMINT NEVER REPRODUCED IT: low concurrency, no accumulated stuck operations, and no long-lived retry backlog. The solomint gateway serves 4 federations with real traffic and a day's worth of stuck ops.

RULED OUT ALONG THE WAY: clock skew (guardian/node/local agree to the second, matches Cloudflare Date); transport hang (res_ok=true); v2 modules and small amounts (local matrix: 4-guardian +/- mintv2/walletv2 AND 1-guardian +v2, at 1000 sat and 20 sat, all CLAIMED); route-blinding; mint denominations; max_notes_per_denomination; tx balance; ingress timeouts; guardian health.

STILL OPEN: why the FIRST submission of a given operation fails to be observed in the first place. The retry-with-same-notes behaviour is what makes it permanent and self-sustaining, but the initial trigger isn't pinned.

UPSTREAM VALUE: two distinct issues — (a) #5238, the swallowed submit_transaction outcome, which hides everything; (b) NEW: a gateway funding retry must not rebuild a tx reusing already-spent notes (either resubmit the identical tx idempotently, or re-select notes after confirming the prior tx's fate). Worth filing (b) with this evidence.

CLEANUP OWED: revert GitOps debug commits c2b3ef1 (guardian) and 7f90592 (gateway) in zalguod/devops when done.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
OPEN QUESTION LARGELY CLOSED (2026-07-19): state machines ACCUMULATE per stuck operation — direct evidence from the gateway's own client DB.

METHOD: newly-joined federations do NOT get a {federation_id}.db dir (those are "legacy"); their client DB is isolated INSIDE gatewayd.db under prefix [ClientDatabase=0x10][federation_id] (gateway/fedimint-gateway-server-db/src/lib.rs get_client_database, and gateway/fedimint-gateway-server/src/client.rs build() else-branch). Copied the rocksdb files out of the pod (876K, live DB untouched) and inspected with fedimint-dbtool. ActiveStateKey encodes operation_id FIRST (fedimint-client-module/src/sm/executor.rs), so active SMs can be grouped by operation directly from key bytes.

SOLOMINT CLIENT DB (625 keys): a1 ActiveStates=34, a2 InactiveStates=63, 2c/2d OperationLog=11 ops, 39 EventLog=194.

ACTIVE STATE MACHINES PER OPERATION:
  11  op=7e80b3a1...6d265d9f   (my first 10-sat invoice, ~1 day old)
   7  op=0dfb1e94...a30bfb9b   (20-sat Fedi attempt)
   5  op=ae5cf4d1...700fd943   (15-sat Henwen attempt)
   4  op=836e09c6...92c3229e
   4  op=50e55ee3...5d14bc08   (TODAY'S FRESH test payment)
   1  each: a0cbb6c5, 687d157c, 34bc5080

A healthy lnv2 receive needs ~3 SMs (Receive + Complete + one TxSubmission). 11 active on one operation is unambiguous accumulation. Every accumulating op is one of my stuck receives. 50e55ee3 is TODAY'S payment and already has 4 — so accumulation starts immediately, it is NOT merely a restart artifact.

MECHANISM (now end-to-end evidenced):
  SMs never reach a terminal state -> they accumulate on the operation -> the sm-executor re-drives ALL of them (observed: 8 submissions in a ~2s burst, all from spawn{task="sm-executor"}, only 2 HTLC intercepts in the same window) -> each builds/submits a transaction over the SAME notes -> one wins, the rest are rejected "The note is already spent" -> the rejection is not observed (#5238) -> repeat. Once the contract's 1h expiry passes, submissions additionally fail "The contract is expired" and the receive is permanently dead.

RULED OUT this round: TOCTOU on relay_incoming_htlc's operation_exists guard (only 2 intercepts vs 9 submissions — the resubmissions are executor-driven, not intercept-driven); TxSubmissionStatesSM rebuilding (it holds Created(tx) and is terminal on Accepted/Rejected); ReceiveStateMachine retrying (Funding only awaits decryption shares; Rejected/Success/Failure are terminal).

REMAINING SUB-QUESTION: why the SMs never reach terminal state — i.e. why the Created -> Rejected transition in fedimint-client-module/src/transaction/sm.rs does not fire and retire the SM. That is now a narrow, code-local question rather than an open-ended field investigation.

ARTIFACTS: gateway client DB copy at <scratch>/gwdb (read-only, safe). Upstream issue filed: fedimint/fedimint#8834.

comments.author: master
master
comments.text:
CORRECTION #3 (2026-07-19) — the premise I put in fedimint#8834 ("transaction accepted-by-peer but never reaches consensus") is NOT SUPPORTED. Correcting before it misleads anyone.

WHAT DISPROVED IT: distinct-txid accounting on the guardian (not log-line counts, which double-count resubmissions):
  received distinct = 16   (17 log lines)
  rejected distinct =  9   (10 log lines)
  accepted distinct =  7
  9 + 7 = 16, and `comm` of received-vs-(accepted|rejected) is EMPTY -> ZERO trapped transactions.
Every transaction the guardian received got a verdict. Also 0 occurrences of "Unable to submit the tx into consensus", so the discarded `submission_sender.send()` result is not firing either.

WHAT IS ACTUALLY ESTABLISHED: the client's TxSubmission state machines are NOT RETIRED even though their transactions resolve. From the gateway client DB (ActiveStateMeta.created_at, decoded from the a1 values; module discriminator fdffff = varint 0xffff = the transaction-submission pseudo-module):
  active tx-submission SMs at snapshot 21:34Z:
    1 x created 2026-07-19 02:43:07Z   <-- ~19 HOURS OLD, still active
    8 x created 2026-07-19 21:08:57-59Z (the executor burst)
    1 x created 2026-07-19 21:10:33Z   (fresh test payment)
The 19-hour-old active SM is the smoking gun: whatever its transaction's fate, it should have terminated long ago.

SO THE CHAIN IS: transactions resolve guardian-side -> client SMs are not retired -> they accumulate (34 active total, ~10 of them tx-submission, 11 on one operation) -> executor re-drives them -> each submits a transaction over the same notes -> all but one rejected "note is already spent" -> invisible (#5238) -> repeat.

STILL OPEN (genuinely): WHY the SMs are not retired when their transaction resolves. Ruled out so far: no rebuild path in ReceiveStateMachine or relay_incoming_htlc (idempotent via operation_exists); TxSubmissionStates::Created is terminal on Accepted/Rejected and its submit loop resubmits the SAME txid; await_tx_accepted DOES resolve Err on Rejected; the notifier is NOT racy (subscribe() starts the live broadcast first, THEN replays BOTH active and inactive states from the DB); clock skew; transport (res_ok=true); v2 modules and small amounts (local devimint matrix all CLAIMED).

METHOD NOTE FOR NEXT TIME: count DISTINCT txids, never log lines — resubmission double-counts and produced a false "everything balances" reading earlier, and a false "nothing balances" reading before that. Verify at the object level, not the line level.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
INFRA GATE IS NOT MET — retracting the 2026-07-18 "GO for money-path dogfood" call (2026-07-20).

ROOT CAUSE: solomint is a SINGLE-GUARDIAN (1-of-1) federation, and fedimint's lnv2 threshold decryption panics at n=1. crypto/tpe/src/lib.rs:211 — lagrange_multipliers() filters out the only scalar, reduce() on the empty iterator returns None, and .expect("We have at least one share") fires. The empty product should be 1. Filed upstream as fedimint/fedimint#8837 with a deterministic repro (existing test_roundtrip at PEERS=THRESHOLD=1) and a verified one-line fix (.fold(Scalar::ONE, |a,b| a*b) — makes both the 1-of-1 and the existing 4-of-3 test pass).

WHY IT BLOCKS EVERYTHING: the panic happens inside a state-machine transition, so it kills the client's sm-executor task ("Task shut down uncleanly name=sm-executor"). After that the gateway keeps ACCEPTING work — intercepts HTLCs, builds funding txs, writes TxSubmission SMs, logs "Finalized and submitting transaction" — but nothing drives them. Proven from the gateway client DB: 11 active TxSubmission SMs spanning 26h, none ever retired, while the guardian received ZERO submit_transaction calls. Restart drives a burst (54 submissions, one tx accepted) then panics again within seconds. Reproduced on every restart.

WHAT IS NOW PROVEN GOOD (don't re-litigate): the payment path itself works. A real mainnet payment from marigold via the Henwen gateway routed to ostracoda, the HTLC was intercepted (amount_msat=10000), and the gateway built a correct funding tx: 4 mint inputs -> lnv2 Incoming of 7950 msat (net-of-fee, confirming the 3d5 received_msat premise). Cost ~12 sat, refunds at contract expiry.

TWO EARLIER CLAIMS IN THIS BEAD ARE WRONG:
- "destination is an lnv2 blinded-path key" — NO. lncli decodepayreq shows a plain bolt11 to ostracoda's node 02dfecc8, route_hints:[], route-blinding bit 25 NOT required. Any LN node with a route to ostracoda can pay it; the 2026-07-18 "no viable payment source" conclusion was wrong, and Henwen proves it.
- "client SMs are not retired" (CORRECTION #3) — NO. That was inferred from log absence. The SMs are not retired because the executor is DEAD, not because retirement is broken.

NEXT: 7qc money legs need a MULTI-GUARDIAN lnv2 federation (4-guardian devimint already claimed cleanly in the earlier matrix), or a locally-patched fedimint with the #8837 fix. Do NOT spend more time on solomint for money legs — it is structurally incapable until #8837 ships. The read-only/probe half of acceptance remains valid.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
INFRA GATE NOW GENUINELY MET (2026-07-20) — supersedes the retraction earlier today.

Root cause of the block was fedimint's tpe crate panicking on a 1-of-1 federation
(crypto/tpe/src/lib.rs:211), which killed the gateway client's sm-executor and silently
wedged every state machine. Filed as fedimint#8837, fixed in PR #8838 (+ backport #8839),
validated in devimint at FM_FED_SIZE=1 (unpatched: all refund paths pass, first successful
payment panics and hangs 47min; patched: every payment path passes), and DEPLOYED to the
ostracoda gateway as a patched image pinned by digest
(registry.galtland.network/library/gatewayd:v0.11.1-tpe-642f3ed8, GitOps b862ee5).

LIVE PROOF on solomint after the deploy — a real mainnet lnv2 payment completed end to end:
  buyer (marigold) -> Henwen gateway -> ostracoda -> HTLC intercepted -> lnv2 incoming
  contract funded 7950 msat net-of-fee -> guardians threshold-decrypted the preimage ->
  HTLC settled ("Success" + preimage returned to payer) -> receiver claimed ("Claimed").
  Balance 4606 -> 11038 msat. Gateway: 0 tpe panics, 0 unclean shutdowns, 1137 sm-executor
  lines (was 0 across 7.5h), 140 submit_transaction at the guardian (was 0).

Separately, the RefundedBundle wrong-txid cascade (fedimint#8834) is now fixed in
releases/v0.11 via merged backport #8836 (ca6d26b); lnrent's pin carries the tpe fix at
douglaz/fedimint@642f3ed8.

STILL TO DO for 7qc acceptance — all four legs remain unrun, and note today's proof was a
RAW fedimint payment, not driven through lnrentd:
  (1) full buyer golden path via the daemon, (2) refund arm (late-order AND failed-provision
  x LN-address AND LNURL), (3) restart recovery (settlement window + refund in flight),
  (4) one operator sweep.

PRACTICAL GAP: money is thin. Buyer wallet (marigold) 93875 msat, operator/receiver
(solomint client) 11038 msat, and each payment burns ~2050 msat in gateway fees. A full run
needs several orders plus refunds; top up before starting.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LEG 2 LATE-ORDER TRIGGER — **NOT BLACK-BOX REACHABLE**, and I believe the acceptance criterion
should be amended rather than the leg claimed. Evidence, not opinion:

WHAT I DID: placed order 7qc-late-1, deliberately left it UNPAID through its full 60-minute invoice
window, and confirmed the daemon did its half correctly — reconcile expired the invoice and moved
the sub to EXPIRED. Then attempted to pay the expired bolt11 from the school wallet.

RESULT: the PAYER refuses before a payment is ever attempted:
  fedimint-cli module ln pay <expired bolt11>  ->  {"error": "Invoice has expired"}

WHY THIS IS STRUCTURAL, not a quirk of this wallet: the capture gate keys off the invoice's
`expires_at`, which capture.rs:45 documents as "the bolt11/reservation expiry". So reaching
Capture::RefundDue by this route requires a settlement that ARRIVES AFTER the bolt11 has expired —
but a conforming payer will not pay an expired bolt11 at all. The only ways in are (a) a payment
already in flight when expiry passes (a genuine but unschedulable race — slow route / held HTLC),
or (b) the unmatched-settlement path, which needs a settlement for an external_id the daemon never
issued. Neither can be arranged deliberately from outside without faking a settlement, and faking
one would prove nothing about the real path.

WHAT IS ACTUALLY COVERED: the gate is defensive code for that race, and it IS tested. Ran them:
  capture::tests::order_settlement_at_or_after_expiry_refunds_not_provisions ... ok
  capture::tests::order_settlement_before_expiry_provisions ................. ok
  capture::tests::order_settlement_with_null_expiry_provisions .............. ok
  capture::tests::settlement_on_an_expired_invoice_refunds_once ............. ok
  capture::tests::unmatched_settlement_records_a_refund_intent .............. ok
  (18 passed, 0 failed)

PROPOSAL: 7qc's criterion "(a) late-order / failed-provision REFUND_DUE" should be read as
SATISFIED by the failed-provision trigger, which is the common one and is PROVEN LIVE TWICE this
run (7qc-golden-2 and 7qc-refundcrash-1, both -> REFUND_DUE -> refund SENT -> sub REFUNDED). The
late-order trigger should be explicitly recorded as unit-test-covered and not live-reachable,
rather than left as an open leg that no amount of dogfooding can close.

I am NOT closing this unilaterally — flagging the reasoning for the operator's call.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CLOSING RATIONALE (2026-07-21, grill session). This bead authorizes deleting lnv1 (lnrent-8ym), so
the standard and the evidence are stated explicitly rather than implied.

STANDARD APPLIED (operator's call): 7qc must prove lnv2 REPLACES lnv1 on the paths a real Operator
hits. Defensive branches that are unreachable from outside count as covered when unit-tested. It is
NOT "every acceptance line demonstrated live" — one line turns out to be undemonstrable by any
conforming payer (below), so that reading could never close.

LEGS — ALL SATISFIED:
1. FULL BUYER GOLDEN PATH — PROVEN LIVE (mainnet solomint + real DigitalOcean). discover -> order ->
   pay real ecash -> lnv2 settle -> capture -> real droplet -> credentials over Nostr DM -> buyer
   SSH'd in (Debian 13.5, 1 vCPU/967MB matching the advertised resources) -> cancel -> ran out the
   paid period -> reconcile TERMINATED + destroyed the droplet. Verified after: droplet gone from
   DO, `lnrent teardowns` = none owed, `lnrent money` = READY / 0 liabilities.
2. REFUND ARM — failed-provision trigger PROVEN LIVE TWICE (7qc-golden-2, 7qc-refundcrash-1): both
   REFUND_DUE -> refund SENT (attempts=1) -> sub REFUNDED. LNURL resolver PROVEN LIVE, and verified
   against a THIRD PARTY: each `resolved_bolt11` is byte-identical to what recurringd independently
   serves at /lnv1/paycodes/recipient/<root_key>/generated/{1,2}.
   - LN-ADDRESS form: satisfied without a live run. `Resolver::resolve` converges both forms at
     refund_resolver.rs:571 — the ONLY LnAddress-specific surface is a pure `format!` URL builder —
     and `lnurl_pay_happy_path_returns_bolt11` already drives the FULL resolve() using a Lightning
     address against a real (in-process) HTTP server: GET -> payRequest -> callback ->
     description_hash -> bolt11 amount + expiry. The whole shared HTTP/LUD-06 envelope was then
     proven live via LNURL. What is genuinely unproven is PROVIDER DIVERSITY (one server, recurringd)
     — identical risk for both forms, and a separate concern from this bead.
   - LATE-ORDER trigger: NOT REACHABLE black-box, and this is structural, not a gap in effort. The
     gate keys off the invoice's `expires_at`, documented at capture.rs:45 as "the bolt11/reservation
     expiry"; a conforming payer refuses an expired bolt11 outright (`fedimint-cli module ln pay` ->
     {"error": "Invoice has expired"}). Reaching it needs a payment already in flight when expiry
     passes (an unschedulable race) or a settlement for an external_id we never issued. Covered by 5
     passing unit tests in capture.rs (order_settlement_at_or_after_expiry_refunds_not_provisions,
     order_settlement_before_expiry_provisions, order_settlement_with_null_expiry_provisions,
     settlement_on_an_expired_invoice_refunds_once, unmatched_settlement_records_a_refund_intent).
     Satisfied under the standard above.
3. RESTART RECOVERY — BOTH cases PROVEN LIVE, using SIGKILL (a crash, not a clean drain):
   (a) settlement window — daemon killed, invoice paid with NO daemon running, restarted: settlement
       recovered, droplet provisioned, creds delivered, buyer SSH'd in. Note the recovery came via
       the lnv2 backend's live watch() re-subscribe, NOT the catch-up counter (which logged
       "settlements caught up count=0") — right outcome, different route than the counter implies.
   (b) refund in flight — daemon killed the instant REFUND_DUE was logged. State at the crash, read
       from sqlite with the daemon down: status=PENDING, attempts=0, no resolution, no payment id.
       After restart: "boot recovery: refunds driven rep=RefundReport { sent: 1, retried: 0,
       failed: 0 }", final row SENT/attempts=1. EXACTLY ONE payment attempt — not zero (dropped
       liability), not two (double-pay). Scope honesty: this is the PENDING/unresolved window, the
       widest and likeliest; it is NOT a crash mid-lnv2-send, which remains unit-test-only.
4. OPERATOR SWEEP — PROVEN LIVE with buyer-side receipt. Quoted from the LEDGER ALONE
   (Earned 89694 / Reserved 0 / Paid out 50000 / Surplus 39694 msat; verdict ALLOW), dry-run by
   default, executed with --yes -> "Sweep SENT: 20 sat"; destination claimed it ({"status":"paid"});
   operator wallet 47710 -> 22110 msat = exactly the quoted 25600 outlay; reconcile OK.

PREFLIGHT GATE (pre-existing criterion): `lnrent preflight` PASS on all four checks including the
real DO token; `lnrent money` Federation ok / Gateway ok / READY.

FOUND AND FIXED EN ROUTE: DigitalOcean had retired `debian-12-x64`, the shipped recipe default, so
every order 422'd at droplet create and cascaded to destroy + REFUND_DUE. Fixed to debian-13-x64
(PR #49). The failure CLASS — provisioning params that pass every launch gate and only fail after a
buyer has paid — is filed as lnrent-1sr (P1), whose design was fixed in this same session (an
optional recipe-declared `preflight` hook, because the defaults live in the hook and a daemon-side
copy would drift).

ALSO FILED FROM THIS RUN: lnrent-4fd (old ecc wallet's recurring sync hangs, ~75 sat unclaimed; the
LNURL path itself is proven working on a fresh wallet), lnrent-rwv (dogfood wallet hygiene),
lnrent-e96 (return the fedimint pin + gatewayd image upstream once the tpe fix ships), ADR-0019
(refund liability = net wallet credit; the Buyer bears both Lightning fees).

MONEY: ~250 sat total across the whole run; two droplets provisioned and torn down; zero owed
teardowns; reconcile OK at every checkpoint.

=> CLOSED. lnrent-8ym is authorized to proceed, with the pin criterion amended (see the 8ym comment:
the fork pin now carries the tpe 1-of-1 fix and must NOT be returned to the upstream v0.11.1 tag
until upstream ships it — lnrent-e96 owns that).

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CORRECTION TO THE CLOSING RATIONALE — MY "LATE-ORDER IS BLACK-BOX UNREACHABLE" ARGUMENT IS WRONG.
Two independent adversarial reviews refuted it and I verified the refutation. Recording it here
because this bead's closure authorizes deleting lnv1, and one of its criteria was declared satisfied
on this false premise.

WHAT I CLAIMED: the late-order REFUND_DUE trigger cannot be reached by a conforming payer, because
the gate keys off the bolt11 expiry and any conforming payer refuses an expired bolt11. I concluded
it was defensive-only, unit-test covered, and satisfied under the agreed standard.

WHY IT IS WRONG: `settled_at` is NOT the payment time. For a live lnv2 settlement it is lnrent's own
wall clock at the moment the daemon OBSERVES the terminal state — `lnv2_backend.rs:1341`
`let settled_at = if live { Some(clock.now()) } else { None };` — taken AFTER `await_receive_final`
returns Claimed and AFTER the `claimed_credit_msat` round-trip. The invoice's `expires_at` is stamped
at mint time (`lnv2_backend.rs:1015`, `INVOICE_EXPIRY_S = 3600`). The gate then fires on
`s.settled_at >= exp` (`capture.rs:184-189`).

So with L = claim-observation latency (gateway forward -> federation funds the contract -> claim tx
accepted -> credit decoded; seconds of consensus), a Buyer who pays a PERFECTLY VALID, UNEXPIRED
bolt11 within L of the expiry boundary is refunded rather than provisioned. No hostile behaviour, no
clock skew, no non-conforming payer — just paying in the last few seconds of the hour.

Worse, this is DOCUMENTED AS INTENDED and I read past it: `backends.rs:36-39` says the design exists
"so a late LIVE payment refunds (capture's g5p gate) instead of being stamped just-in-window and
wrongly provisioned (lnrent-zwk)". The recovery path is the one that is guarded (`recovered_settled_at`
caps at `expires_at - 1`); the live path deliberately is not. There is also a second door: reconcile
independently flips the invoice OPEN -> EXPIRED on its tick, after which a settlement takes capture's
terminal arm to the same RefundDue.

STATUS OF THE LEG: the late-order trigger is REACHABLE and was NOT exercised live. It remains covered
by 5 passing unit tests in capture.rs, and the OTHER refund trigger (failed-provision) was proven live
twice. But "black-box unreachable, therefore satisfied" is not a valid reason and is withdrawn.

IT IS ALSO NOW TESTABLE, which it would not have been under my wrong model: place an order, wait until
just inside the expiry boundary, and pay so the claim lands after it. The window is L wide (seconds),
so expect retries — but it is a real test, not an impossible one.

FOR THE OPERATOR TO DECIDE: whether this reopens 7qc. Arguments both ways —
  - REOPEN: a reachable, real-world-triggerable money path was declared covered on a false premise,
    and this bead is the authorization to delete lnv1.
  - LEAVE CLOSED: the agreed standard was "prove lnv2 REPLACES lnv1 on the paths a real Operator
    hits"; this path is equally reachable and equally untested on lnv1, so lnv2 is not made worse by
    the deletion. The refund MACHINERY it routes into was proven live twice.
I am NOT making that call unilaterally. Filed as a follow-up so it cannot be lost either way.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
OPERATOR DECISION (2026-07-21): 7qc STAYS CLOSED and lnv1 deletion (lnrent-8ym) PROCEEDS.

The late-order refutation above does not change the verdict. The agreed standard is "prove lnv2
REPLACES lnv1 on the paths a real Operator hits" — and the late-order path is equally reachable and
equally untested on lnv1, so deleting lnv1 does not make it worse. The refund machinery that path
routes into was proven live twice on lnv2 (7qc-golden-2, 7qc-refundcrash-1: REFUND_DUE -> SENT with
attempts=1 -> REFUNDED).

The correction stands on the record so the rationale is not mistaken for verified fact, and the
untested-but-reachable path is owned by lnrent-cnf (exercise live + measure claim latency L) against
the lnv2 backend. It is a follow-up, not a gate.

title: Pay recovery: enumerate ALL unrecorded ops per key (multi-live-candidate residual)
description:
# Recovery can only re-point the single-op pay row at ONE unrecorded operation

**From the lnrent-kum adversarial review (its second P1).** With MULTIPLE live unrecorded operations under one idempotency key — two consecutive same-key crash windows (crash after pay_bolt11_invoice, before pay_idx_upsert, twice) — recover_pay_from_oplog can re-point the FAILED row at only ONE of them (whichever the wall-clock scan meets first). If that one later fails definitively, the OTHER stays hidden while the driver starts a fresh generation: the double-pay window reopens for that rare-squared case.

## Scope
Full closure needs per-key operation ENUMERATION: e.g. a fedimint_pay_orphans side table (key, op, kind) populated by the recovery scan for every non-ledger-dead unrecorded op beyond the row's one, with the drivers (or pay_inner's FAILED arm) refusing to start a NEW op while any orphan is unresolved, draining orphans via re-await first. Must not disturb the kum dead-op-ledger decision (fedimint_pay_dead_op) or the y4m.16 classification.

Also from the same review round 3 (upstream hazards, recorded for posterity): OperationLogEntry::outcome() PANICS on a deserialization mismatch (try_outcome().expect) — never read cached pay outcomes without try_outcome(); and PROBING an op via subscribe_* can POISON the outcome cache (the caching wrapper writes the last emitted update as the outcome on an EOF-without-terminal). The kum cut avoids both by using only the local dead-op ledger.
close_reason: done

comments.author: master
comments.text:
DEFERRED after a full reachability analysis (2026-07-16, with the operator) — the fail-closed bail stays as the resting state; the rb-lite implementation attempt (1735 lines, 6 review rounds, non-converging) was discarded unmerged.

Why deferred, not fixed:
1. Same-invoice double-pay is already impossible inside one fedimint client: pay_bolt11_invoice keeps a per-payment-hash attempt counter with a completed-payment short-circuit and an active-attempt bail (fedimint v0.11.1 lib.rs:1307/1312), so two live attempts on one invoice cannot coexist.
2. A second invoice for the same refund key only exists if the resolver re-resolves, which is gated on the persisted invoice being EXPIRED and the row reading a DEFINITIVE failure (ug8 + y4m.16: ambiguous outcomes pin the row PENDING and re-await the same op).
3. Two simultaneously-LIVE unrecorded ops under one key additionally require recover_pay_from_oplog to have MISSED the first op on the intermediate restart — i.e. the oplog wall-clock pathology (backward clock jump hiding entries) or pre-kum-ledger history, exactly what the bail message already says.
4. Even then, both settling requires an HTLC dispatched seconds before invoice expiry racing a re-resolution.

So the bead protects a clock-pathology x double-crash-window x expiry-race stack whose existing handling is already money-safe (refuse to start + operator runbook). Machinery cost (a side table + drain gate in the most dangerous code path) exceeds the benefit; the 6-round rb-lite thrash was a symptom of that inverted cost/benefit.

Partial root-cause fix filed separately: upstream fedimint reorders pay_bolt11_invoice's expiry check below the idempotency checks, so a crash-recovering client can learn its previous attempt's outcome (or live op id) even after the invoice lapses. See the fedimint-upstream bead.

If this ever fires in the field: the runbook in the bail message stands (verify each op via fedimint oplog/gateway logs, INSERT the dead ones into fedimint_pay_dead_op, restart).

title: Upstream fedimint: reorder pay_bolt11_invoice expiry check below the idempotency checks
description:
pay_bolt11_invoice (fedimint-ln-client lib.rs, v0.11.1 lines 1294-1322) checks invoice expiry BEFORE its two per-payment-hash idempotency oracles: the completed-payment short-circuit (returns the finished OutgoingLightningPayment) and the active-attempt bail (returns PreviousPaymentAttemptStillInProgress carrying the live operation_id).

That ordering blocks exactly the crash-recovery case: a client that crashed after committing a pay op, recovering after the invoice lapsed, calls pay_bolt11_invoice(same invoice) to learn what happened — and gets 'Invoice has expired' instead of the completed payment or the live op id. For lnrent this is mitigated by oplog extra_meta recovery (lnrent-4gt/kum), but the fix belongs upstream: reorder to completed -> active -> expiry. Semantics: expired+completed returns the payment; expired+active returns the in-progress error with the op id; expired+fresh still refuses. Non-expired flows byte-identical. No legitimate caller depends on refusing an expired invoice before the idempotency checks.

Deliverable: PR against fedimint/fedimint master (patch drafted locally first, cargo check/test on fedimint-ln-client). Origin: the lnrent-7so deferral analysis (2026-07-16).

- Reorder in pay_bolt11_invoice: completed-payment short-circuit -> active-attempt bail -> expiry ensure (was expiry-first).
- New integration test returns_completed_payment_for_expired_invoice_already_paid: VERIFIED non-vacuous (fails on unpatched master with 'Invoice has expired', passes with the patch).
- rejects_expired_invoice + cannot_pay_same_{internal,external}_invoice_twice all green (4/4, 9.5s).

title: Adopt PreviousPaymentAttemptStillInProgress in start_new_pay (lazy crash-window heal)
description:
Enabled by the v0.11.1-pay-idempotency client pin (fedimint/fedimint#8818 backport, lnrent-7y1).

TODAY: a crash window (op committed, fedimint_pay row missing) inside a daemon run leaves pay(key) -> absent row -> start_new_pay -> pay_bolt11_invoice -> Err(PreviousPaymentAttemptStillInProgress{operation_id}) -> generic '.context(initiating refund payment)' failure -> the refund driver retries every cycle and hits the same error until a RESTART lets recover_pay_from_oplog backfill the row (startup-only heal). A liveness gap, not a money bug.

FIX: in start_new_pay, downcast the pay error to fedimint_ln_client::PayBolt11InvoiceError::PreviousPaymentAttemptStillInProgress and ADOPT the operation: pay_idx_upsert(key, op_hex, PENDING, kind, now) + return the reconstructed PayType so pay_inner's normal await path re-awaits it (the y4m.16 classification then lands the real outcome). Heals the crash window lazily on the next drive, no restart needed. Kind: the error doesn't carry internal-vs-ln; derive from the PayType the retry would have used, or re-derive via the oplog entry — design carefully.

MONEY-SAFETY: adoption only ever RE-AWAITS an existing op (never starts a second one) — strictly safer than the current error path. Must NOT weaken recover_pay_from_oplog / the kum dead-op ledger / y4m.16 classification: the 2026-07-16 invariants review (lnrent-7y1 verification) PROVED those load-bearing — fedimint's PaymentResult table cannot distinguish an accepted refund (funds back, safe retry) from a REJECTED refund after a gateway claim (recipient paid from operator funds); only our subscribe-stream classification sees that. The startup oplog recovery stays; this is additive liveness only.

Verification: unit test the downcast/adoption seam; integration test simulating the crash window (delete the row after pay, drive again) asserting adoption re-awaits the SAME op and no second op starts.
close_reason: done

comments.author: master
comments.text: Design LOCKED by the 2026-07-17 plan-eng-review (all decisions operator-approved): [2A] kind from OPLOG META via operation_log().get_operation point lookup (reuse recovery's is_internal_payment expression; NEVER the retry-time PayType heuristic); downcast_ref through the .context chain (verified feasible); extract the PENDING-arm PayType reconstruction into a shared helper. [8A] KEY-MATCH GATE: adoption requires meta.extra_meta.lnrent_idempotency_key == retrying key; mismatch -> fail-closed + warn (cross-order same-invoice collision; sibling Ok-shortcut surface filed as its own bead). [7A] NO-PARK on cap failure in the retry path: plain error, row untouched (closes the verified intra-run double-refund window: FAILED-park over a live op -> generation re-resolve); parse-error parks stay. [4A] BOTH arms in scope: absent-row AND stale-FAILED (overwrite = lazy ReplaceStaleFailed, kum-consistent, dead-op ledger untouched — document the equivalence). [6A] honest framing: fail-closed meta edge is UNCHANGED-from-today (7so/runbook class), reachable heal scenario is intra-run index-write failure without crash. [5C+9A] verification: 9 CI tests (incl. key-mismatch + no-park) + fedimint_live 8a (settled-rowless heals via completed shortcut) + 8b (HOLD invoice keeps op ACTIVE, adoption-arm-FIRED assertion) — both proven red without their code. title: Delete lnv1: fedimint_backend lnv1 paths + ADR-0017 machinery + deps + pin return
description:
ADR-0018 (operator directive 2026-07-17: lnv1 deleted ASAP; greenfield — no migration, lnv1 never operator-facing). Execute AFTER the 7qc dogfood passes on the lnv2 backend.

Deletion list: fedimint_backend.rs lnv1 pay/receive paths incl. recover_pay_from_oplog + recover_index_from_oplog (lnv1 halves), the kum fedimint_pay_dead_op ledger, pay_recovery_action, y4m.16 classify_ln_pay_state/classify_internal_pay_state + await_pay machinery, y4m.15/19 index GC where lnv1-shaped, extra_meta key embedding, their tests (~2k lines total); drop fedimint-ln-client + fedimint-ln-common deps; RETURN the ten dep pins from douglaz/fedimint v0.11.1-pay-idempotency to the upstream v0.11.1 TAG (the #8818 backport served lnv1 only; #8818 remains a valid upstream PR for lnv1 users). Keep whatever index tables the lnv2 backend actually needs (its deterministic op ids shrink the requirement — decide in the lnv2-backend bead).

Docs: ADR-0017 gets a status note ('recovery machinery deleted with lnv1, per ADR-0018' — the ADR stays as history/method reference); sweep MEMORY.md for stale lnv1-machinery references. Mind the rustfmt-cascade gotcha when touching lib.rs module decls.
## Acceptance Criteria
- grep confirms ZERO remaining references to: recover_pay_from_oplog, fedimint_pay_dead_op, pay_recovery_action, classify_ln_pay_state/classify_internal_pay_state, await_pay lnv1 machinery, lnrent_idempotency_key extra_meta embedding (lnv1 form).
- fedimint-ln-client + fedimint-ln-common removed from Cargo.toml; the ten dep pins returned to the upstream v0.11.1 TAG. Lockfile criterion (audit I5): cargo tree proves fedimint-ln-client/-ln-common are ABSENT; remaining fedimint deps resolve from the upstream v0.11.1 source; no unintended version bumps; the full lockfile delta is EXPLAINED in the PR (crate removals legitimately change the resolved graph — exact-delta matching is not required).
- ADR-0017 carries the status note ("recovery machinery deleted with lnv1, per ADR-0018"); MEMORY.md swept for stale lnv1-machinery references.
- cargo test --workspace + clippy green with --features fedimint AND --no-default-features; PR through standard gates.
- Blocked on lnrent-7qc (lnv2 acceptance green) AND lnrent-ccr (dogfood wallet drained + obligations clear, operator-confirmed) — do not start before either.

Verified clean by hand + local codex/fable (no over/under-deletion, Bolt11Invoice determinism preserved). Doc-sweep done. Follow-ups: lnrent-2ad (P1 backup test), lnrent-o4k (P2 gateway field).

comments.author: master
comments.text:
PIN-RETURN CRITERION AMENDED (2026-07-21, grill session). The original criterion — "the ten dep pins
returned to the upstream v0.11.1 TAG" — rested on "the #8818 backport served lnv1 only". That is no
longer true and following it literally would REGRESS lnv2.

Since 2026-07-20 the `douglaz/fedimint v0.11.1-pay-idempotency` pin sits at 642f3ed8, which carries
BOTH:
  (a) the #8818 pay-idempotency backport — lnv1 only, and genuinely deletable with lnv1; and
  (b) the tpe `lagrange_multipliers` 1-of-1 fix (upstream #8837 / PR #8838, backport #8839) — which
      serves LNV2 on ANY single-guardian federation.

Without (b), aggregating a single decryption share panics, and the panic kills the client's
`sm-executor` task so nothing is ever submitted again. That is every aggregation on a 1-of-1
federation — including solomint, the staging federation 7qc's entire live acceptance ran on.

Upstream has NOT shipped it: #8838 is OPEN (unmerged), #8839 is a DRAFT. There is no upstream
v0.11.x tag containing the fix, so "return to the upstream v0.11.1 tag" is not currently reachable
without breaking lnv2.

AMENDED CRITERION for 8ym: drop `fedimint-ln-client` + `fedimint-ln-common` and delete the lnv1
machinery as planned, but KEEP the douglaz fork pin. Rewrite the pin's in-repo justification from
"#8818 backport for lnv1" to "carries the tpe 1-of-1 fix until upstream ships it" — otherwise the
next reader deletes a pin whose remaining purpose is undocumented. The lockfile criterion is
unchanged except that fedimint-* legitimately still resolves from the fork, not the tag.

The eventual return of BOTH the Cargo pin and the deployed gatewayd image is owned by a NEW bead
(see dependents) and is NOT part of 8ym's definition of done.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
RETRACTION — MY EARLIER PIN AMENDMENT ON THIS BEAD WAS WRONG. Two independent adversarial reviews
refuted it, and I verified the refutation myself. The ORIGINAL criterion stands: 8ym SHOULD return
the ten dep pins to the upstream v0.11.1 tag.

WHAT I CLAIMED: that 642f3ed8 carries the tpe 1-of-1 fix which "serves LNV2 on ANY single-guardian
federation", so returning the pin at 8ym would regress lnv2 and re-break solomint.

WHY IT IS WRONG: lnrent never calls the panicking function. At v0.11.1,
`git grep aggregate_dk_shares` has exactly ONE non-test caller —
`modules/fedimint-gwv2-client/src/receive_sm.rs:210`, the GATEWAY's lnv2 client.
`fedimint-lnv2-client` (what lnrent uses) imports only `AggregateDecryptionKey` and `derive_agg_dk`:
the RECEIVER derives the aggregate key from its own encryption seed and never aggregates guardian
decryption shares. And `fedimint-gwv2-client` is NOT in lnrent's dependency tree — no entry in
Cargo.toml, none in Cargo.lock.

So the tpe panic lives in the GATEWAY process (ostracoda), never in lnrentd. That is also exactly
where we observed it, and it is why deploying a patched gatewayd image fixed the symptom while
lnrent's own pin was irrelevant to it. Corroborating evidence we already had and I failed to weigh:
the 7qc live run completed lnv2 receives against a 1-of-1 federation — which could not have happened
if this panic were on lnrent's own receive path.

CONSEQUENCES:
- The original acceptance criterion is RESTORED: return the ten pins to the upstream v0.11.1 tag as
  part of 8ym. There is no lnv2 regression risk from doing so. (Whether the pin can go all the way
  back also depends on the #8818 pay-idempotency backport being genuinely lnv1-only, which the
  original bead already asserts and which the deletion itself makes moot.)
- lnrent-e96 is NARROWED: it should own ONLY the deployed gatewayd image revert
  (registry.galtland.network/library/gatewayd:v0.11.1-tpe-642f3ed8 -> an upstream tag once a v0.11.x
  ships #8839). The Cargo-pin half of that bead is void — 8ym handles it.
- PR #49's `build(deps)` commit message misattributes the fix ("the panic kills the client's
  sm-executor task") in a way that reads as if lnrent needed it. True of the gateway's client, not
  lnrentd. To be corrected on the PR.

METHOD NOTE for whoever reads this later: I reasoned from "the fix is absent from the tag" plus "we
saw the panic on a 1-of-1 federation" to "lnrent needs the fix", without ever checking whether
lnrent's dependency graph reaches the panicking function. The absent-from-tag half was correct and
verified; the needs-it half was never checked. Verify the CALL PATH, not just the presence of a bug.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
PIN CRITERION REVISED AGAIN (2026-07-22, operator decision): KEEP THE douglaz/fedimint FORK. Do NOT
return the dep pins to the upstream v0.11.1 tag. This SUPERSEDES both the original "return to the
upstream v0.11.1 TAG" criterion AND my 2026-07-21 retraction (which restored that criterion).

VERIFIED (from the fork's own commits, not asserted): douglaz/fedimint @ 642f3ed8 is the upstream

  642f3ed8  fix(tpe): lagrange multiplier for a single share        -> crypto/tpe/...

WHY KEEP IT ANYWAY (the operator's call, and the right one): the fork is standing PATCH INFRASTRUCTURE.

Returning to a frozen upstream tag would be churn for zero functional gain.

crates). Every OTHER fedimint-* pin STAYS on douglaz/fedimint branch v0.11.1-pay-idempotency,

- lnrent-o4k (P2): the `gateway` config field is required at bootstrap but ignored by lnv2.

title: Refund idempotency: completed-payment Ok-short-circuit can mask cross-order same-invoice payouts
description: Pre-existing surface, sibling of the 85t adoption key-match gate (2026-07-17 eng review, Issue 8 / TODO-1). fedimint's per-invoice idempotency returns Ok(completed) for ANY caller re-submitting an already-paid invoice: two lnrent orders sharing one buyer-supplied raw bolt11 could both read SUCCEEDED off a single payout (order B masked by order A's payment). 85t's 8A gate closes this for the ADOPTION path (key-match on extra_meta.lnrent_idempotency_key, fail-closed + warn on mismatch); the Ok(outgoing) arm of start_new_pay retains the surface. Fix directions: (a) key-compare at the Ok arm too (the returned completed payment's op meta carries the original key — same point lookup as 8A), or (b) reject duplicate raw-bolt11 refund destinations at order intake (dedup policy — may be the better home). Requires buyer-reused invoices across orders (unusual/buyer error) — low urgency, silent-under-refund class. Prior art: 85t code comment once landed; the 2026-07-17 plan-eng-review Issue 8 verification.
close_reason: done

comments.author: master
comments.text: CLOSED (2026-07-17 grill Q7): root cause removed by design — order intake will reject raw-bolt11 refund destinations (see the intake bead just filed), so buyer-supplied invoices can never be shared across orders; resolver-minted invoices are per-generation fresh. Residual defense-in-depth lives in lnrent-3d5's [8A] rule: verify any backend dedup answer (lnv2 DuplicatePaymentAttempt) against OUR idempotency key. The lnv1 Ok-short-circuit surface itself is deleted with lnv1 (lnrent-8ym).

title: phoenixd feasibility spike: supplied seed, seed-only restore, retry idempotency, secure deployment contract
description:
Extracted from lnrent-xk3 per the second-model audit (B5/I3). Before ANY backend construction, prove against a REAL phoenixd: (1) an Operator-derived supplied seed (HKDF lnrent:phoenixd:v1 -> BIP39) is accepted at first start (pre-placed seed.dat or equivalent); (2) seed-only recovery through the ACINQ peer actually restores funds (clean-room restore test); (3) payment-status idempotency across every pay crash window — retry after a crash cannot double-pay (document phoenixd's paymentHash/paymentId dedup semantics); (4) same-invoice retry works after definitive failure. DEFINE the secure deployment contract: localhost-plaintext-HTTP-only vs remote-requires-TLS policy; api_password storage + redaction (never logged, never in errors); seed.dat placement atomic + 0600 + O_NOFOLLOW/symlink-refusal (mirror identity.rs); existing-file behavior (never overwrite); safe CLI output for lnrent phoenixd-seed (print only on explicit flag).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each of (1)-(4) demonstrated against a real phoenixd with evidence captured in the bead/PR.
- The deployment contract written up (feeds lnrent-xk3's config validation + docs).
- FAILURE of (1) or (2) => STOP: recorded product re-decision (grill Q3 fallback: separate backup item) via ADR amendment before xk3 proceeds. Failure of (3) => backend design must add its own dedup ledger; record it.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
DESIGN HALF COMPLETE (2026-07-17, autonomous — the live-verification half needs a running phoenixd). Authoritative API read from ACINQ/phoenixd Api.kt. Full design in scratch (phoenixd-design.md); essence:

TRAIT->ENDPOINT MAP: create_invoice->POST /createinvoice (externalId maps DIRECTLY to ADR-0009 external_id; serialized=bolt11; backend_invoice_id=paymentHash). lookup/lookup_settlement->GET /payments/incoming?externalId= or /{paymentHash} (isPaid, receivedSat, fees, completedAt -> the zwk live-vs-recovery settled_at). watch()->GET /websocket payment_received events + reconnect catch-up via /payments/incoming. balance->GET /getbalance balanceSat. pay*->POST /payinvoice (invoice, amountSat; NO idempotency param -> local phoenixd_pay key->(paymentHash,paymentId) map, intent-before-pay). payment_status_by_key-> local map + /payments/outgoing/{uuid} or /payments/outgoingbyhash/{paymentHash} (the crash-recovery seam). failed_refund_can_reuse_invoice()=TRUE (no lnv2 attempt-0 restriction). lnv2_functional_probe=NotApplicable. Auth=HTTP Basic (empty user, http-password from phoenix.conf, full-access scope). Transport: phoenixd serves PLAINTEXT HTTP only -> config must require loopback url OR explicit allow_insecure_remote + operator TLS proxy.

KEY DISCOVERY: phoenixd ALSO takes a receive-side fee (a liquidity/channel-open receive gives receivedSat < invoice amountSat) -> the SAME net-of-fee case as lnv2 -> the received_msat money-core plumbing (3d5, already merged) carries phoenixd unchanged, NO further ledger work.

THE HARD DESIGN QUESTION (flag for the operator): phoenixd does NOT quote fees before paying (payinvoice reports routingFeeSat only AFTER, no maxFee param in the API as read). INV-1 (payout+fee<=received) enforcement options: (a) a maxFeeSat param if a newer phoenixd exposes one; (b) pay a payout reduced by a conservative fee RESERVE, assert the actual fee landed within it, park FAILED+alert if not (never silently over-refund); (c) advisory pricing. Lean = (b), sized from real testnet fee behavior.

SEED (grill Q3-A): HKDF(operator_seed, 'lnrent:phoenixd:v1') -> 16B entropy -> BIP39 12-word -> phoenixd seed.dat; lnrent phoenixd-seed CLI writes it (0600/O_NOFOLLOW/no-overwrite) or --print for remote.

6 MUST-VERIFY against a real phoenixd (blocks xk3): (1) does phoenixd accept a PRE-PLACED supplied seed.dat, or insist on generating? (fallback: separate backup, amend ADR-0018); (2) seed-only restore recovers channels+funds via the ACINQ peer? (3) does payinvoice accept a max-fee bound, else measure real fee magnitude for the reserve; (4) confirm receivedSat<amountSat on a liquidity receive; (5) does payinvoice dedup a repeated same-bolt11 pay or double-pay? (6) is /payments/outgoingbyhash queryable immediately after payinvoice returns (crash recovery)?

comments.author: master
comments.text:
INV-1 MECHANISM DECIDED (2026-07-21 grill session). Supersedes the design lean of option (b)
"reserve + post-hoc assert".

DECISION: max_fee is ONE number used TWICE — it is both the bound passed to phoenixd AND the amount
deducted from the payout.

    payout      = liability - max_fee
    actual_fee <= max_fee                (phoenixd ENFORCES the bound)
    => payout + actual_fee <= liability  INV-1 holds EXACTLY, pre-payment, no post-hoc assertion

This is the same shape as lnv2 (quote the fee, subtract it), not reserve-and-hope.

WHY THE ORIGINAL LEAN (b) WAS REJECTED: its failure path — "assert the actual fee landed within the
reserve, park FAILED + alert if it exceeds" — does not restore anything, because the payment has
already left. Each overrun is money the Operator paid out beyond what that Order credited. And the
BUYER CHOOSES THE REFUND DESTINATION (LN address / LNURL -> their provider's bolt11), so the Buyer
influences the route and therefore the fee. That makes the overrun Buyer-influenceable and turns
repeated forced refunds back into the drain vector ADR-0019 exists to close. A post-hoc assertion is
a detector, not a control.

SIZING: a small configured base + bps of the payout, tuned from REAL phoenixd fee behaviour measured
on testnet (this is what live question 3 must produce — a number, not just a yes/no).

ACCEPTED CONSEQUENCE: because max_fee is deducted rather than the actual fee, a cheap route means the
Buyer was still charged the maximum and the Operator keeps the difference. lnv2 has no such gap (it
deducts a quote). The residual is kept — recovering it would need a second payment with a second fee,
which is absurd at these amounts — and the gap is bounded by keeping max_fee TIGHT. This is now
recorded in ADR-0019 under "Backend-specific: phoenixd".

LIVE QUESTION 3 IS NOW GO/NO-GO, NOT INFORMATIONAL: if phoenixd's payinvoice does NOT accept and
ENFORCE a max-fee bound (maxFeeSat/maxFeePct or equivalent), this construction is impossible and the
question of whether phoenixd may ship automated refunds at all returns to the operator. Verify
ENFORCEMENT, not just parameter acceptance: prove a payment whose only route exceeds the bound FAILS
rather than silently paying more.

Failure path needs no new machinery: a definitive pay failure (no route within max_fee) parks the
refund FAILED and raises the existing RefundParked alert, recoverable via `lnrent refund-retry` —
the Operator then decides whether to pay manually at a fee they accept.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LIVE QUESTION 3 ANSWERED WITHOUT A LIVE PHOENIXD — and the premise it rested on was WRONG.

Verified against the LATEST RELEASE, phoenixd v0.9.0 (tag v0.9.0, published 2026-07-13), read via
the GitHub API at that tag rather than from a working tree:

(1) NO FEE BOUND ON OUTBOUND PAYMENTS. `Api.kt` `post("payinvoice")` takes exactly `invoice` and an
    optional `amountSat` override, then calls `peer.payInvoice(amount, invoice)`. No maxFee param.
    Nor is there a global config bound: phoenixd's fee options (`--max-mining-fee`,
    `--max-relative-fee-percent`, `--max-fee-credit`) all govern ON-CHAIN / automated-liquidity
    fees — a receive-side channel-open concern — not outbound Lightning routing.

(2) BUT THE FEE IS DETERMINISTIC, WHICH IS BETTER THAN A BOUND. `conf/Lsp.kt`:

        private val trampolineFees = listOf(
            TrampolineFees(feeBase = 4.sat, feeProportional = 4_000, cltvExpiryDelta = ...(576))
        )

    A SINGLE tier — no escalating retry ladder — of 4 sat base + 4000 ppm (0.4%). This is the fee
    budget the payment is sent with, so the actual routing fee CANNOT EXCEED it; an attempt needing
    more fails instead of silently costing more.

CONSEQUENCE: the b2f premise "phoenixd does NOT quote fees before paying" is wrong in the way that
matters. The fee is not unbounded-and-unknowable — it is a compile-time constant, structurally the
same as lnv2's published `routing_info` schedule. So the max_fee construction decided earlier works
with INV-1 EXACT, and neither the reserve-and-assert scheme nor a "no automated refunds on phoenixd"
fallback is needed:

    max_fee = 4 sat + 0.4% x payout      (solve with payout = liability - max_fee)
    actual_fee <= max_fee                (enforced by the trampoline fee budget)
    => payout + actual_fee <= liability  INV-1 holds, pre-payment

WHERE THE NUMBER LIVES (decided): operator-CONFIGURABLE, defaulting to the v0.9.0 schedule, with the
config comment naming the phoenixd VERSION and FILE the default was read from
(`conf/Lsp.kt` @ v0.9.0). Preflight asserts the running phoenixd's version matches what the schedule
was verified against and warns loudly on mismatch. NOT a bare hardcoded constant: a future phoenixd
that raises the schedule would otherwise break INV-1 silently, with nothing detecting it — the same
failure shape as the retired `debian-12-x64` slug that passed every gate until a buyer had paid
(lnrent-1sr).

STILL TO VERIFY LIVE (the rest of the 6 are unaffected): whether a payment whose route needs more
than the trampoline budget FAILS cleanly (expected `PaymentFailed`) rather than partially settling,
and the actual observed `routingFeeSat` on a real payment to confirm it lands at or under the
schedule.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LIVE QUESTION 1 ANSWERED FROM SOURCE (phoenixd v0.9.0, `Phoenixd.kt`, read at the release tag).

YES — phoenixd accepts a SUPPLIED seed; it does not insist on generating one. Three routes:

    sealed class SeedSpec { data class Manual(val seed: ByteVector); data class SeedPath(val path: Path) }

    --seed <12 words>     (also via the PHOENIX_SEED env var)
    --seed-path <file>
    default: <datadir>/seed.dat        (via getOrGenerateSeed: read if present, generate if absent)

So the b2f/ADR-0018 design stands: HKDF(operator_seed, 'lnrent:phoenixd:v1') -> 16B entropy ->
BIP39 12 words -> PRE-PLACE as seed.dat, and phoenixd adopts it. The CONTEXT.md "Operator seed"
promise ("one backup covers identity AND the wallet") therefore holds for phoenixd too, and the
grill Q3-A fallback (a separate backup item + ADR-0018 amendment) is NOT needed.

USE THE PRE-PLACED FILE — NOT `--seed` AND NOT `PHOENIX_SEED`. Both alternatives put the operator's
money-bearing seed where other local users can read it: argv is world-readable via /proc/<pid>/cmdline
for the life of the process, and the env block likewise via /proc/<pid>/environ. This is not
theoretical — in this same session I leaked a wallet mnemonic into a session transcript by running
`pgrep -af` against a `fedimint-cli restore --mnemonic ...` process, which is the same exposure with
a different reader. The existing design (a `lnrent phoenixd-seed` CLI writing 0600 / O_NOFOLLOW /
no-overwrite) is the right one; record the argv/env routes as REJECTED with this reason so nobody
"simplifies" to them later. Same reasoning as go-live.md's existing warning against putting
LNRENT_MNEMONIC on the run invocation.

REMAINING LIVE QUESTIONS (still need a running phoenixd — genuinely empirical, not answerable from
source): (2) seed-only restore actually recovers channels+funds via the ACINQ peer; (4) confirm
receivedSat < amountSat on a liquidity-triggering receive; (5) does a repeated same-bolt11 payinvoice
dedup or double-pay; (6) is /payments/outgoingbyhash queryable immediately after payinvoice returns.
Plus, from the fee finding: confirm a payment needing more than the trampoline budget FAILS cleanly
and that observed routingFeeSat lands at or under 4 sat + 0.4%.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
LIABILITY-BASELINE CAVEAT (codex reviewer, PR #50): when phoenixd routes a small inbound payment
entirely to `Part.FeeCredit`, its `receivedSat` STILL counts that credit, but `/getbalance` excludes
it from spendable `balanceSat` and reports it separately as `feeCreditSat`. So the phoenixd backend
must NOT map `receivedSat` straight into `received_msat` (the refund-liability baseline, ADR-0019):
that would make a fee-credit-only order refundable with NO spendable credit behind it, and a forced
refund would then draw down the operator's existing balance — the drain vector ADR-0019 exists to
close. xk3 must baseline the liability on the SPENDABLE credit (received minus any fee-credit
portion), or reject a fee-credit-only receipt. Consistent with this bead's earlier note "feeCreditSat
is separate; do not count it as spendable." Recorded in ADR-0019's phoenixd section.

title: Backend strategy: adopt lnv2 + phoenixd, retire lnv1
description:
The 2026-07-16 upstream API survey (lnrent-7y1 follow-on) found fedimint-lnv2-client already embodies the endgame for our pay-path workarounds, BY DESIGN:

- P1 (crash-window op discovery): lnv2 op id is DETERMINISTIC from the invoice alone (OperationId::from_encodable((invoice, 0u64)), lnv2 lib.rs:559-562) and a repeat send returns DuplicatePaymentAttempt(OperationId) CARRYING the id — a crashed caller re-finds its op by recomputing or re-calling. Would obsolete: extra_meta key embedding, recover_pay_from_oplog's scan, kum multi-candidate handling.
- P2 (truthful terminal, the ADR-0017 hole): lnv2's stream AWAITS refund-tx acceptance and on refund-claim failure RE-CHECKS the preimage via the federation (lib.rs:705-727) -> FinalSendOperationState{Success|Refunded|Failure} (await_final_send_operation_state, lib.rs:739-765). The rejected-refund-after-gateway-claim shape becomes DISTINGUISHABLE upstream. Would let the y4m.16 ambiguous-park consult a truthful final state; kum dead-op ledger likely deletable.
- P4 (3-min pending restart trap): gone — unlimited-retry gateway send + block-height-bounded preimage await (send_sm.rs:193-217, 271-288).
- P3 (outcome-cache poisoning/panic): SHARED with lnv1, small core fix (separate bead).

CRITICAL OPERATIONAL CAVEAT: lnv2 permanently refuses re-attempting the same invoice (attempt index fixed at 0) — a definitively-failed send can NEVER retry the same bolt11; a fresh invoice is required. Fits our ug8 LN-address/LNURL resolver flow (re-resolves per generation) but NOT raw-bolt11 refund destinations (those could never retry after a route failure). Also gated on: the operator's federation(s) running the lnv2 module + gateway support; RocksDB state coexistence; our create-invoice/receive side (FinalReceiveOperationState exists, lib.rs:1068-1095).

Scope: an EVALUATION spec (not implementation): module availability in target federations, the raw-bolt11 retry gap, API mapping for FedimintPayment, which of 4gt/kum/y4m.16/7y1 machinery is deletable vs kept as defense-in-depth, migration/coexistence plan. Origin: ADR-0017 + the lnrent-7y1 upstream survey.
close_reason: done

comments.author: master
comments.text:
RESHAPED by the 2026-07-17 plan-eng-review + OPERATOR STRATEGIC DIRECTION: 'if lnv2 simplifies our code, then we should use it; if the operator doesn't like any lnv2 federation/gateway then we can provide another method, like phoenixd.'

New scope — deliverable docs/specs/backend-strategy.md (NOT a migration eval; the old single-federation framing is superseded):
1. POSTURE: new-operator onboarding requires an lnv2-enabled federation OR phoenixd. Enforcement = FUNCTIONAL preflight probe (extend y4m.9 doctor via urw.4 seam): fedimint -> lnv2 module present AND lnv2-capable gateway attached+reachable (module-present/gateway-absent FAILS); phoenixd -> API reachable + liquidity sanity.
2. PHOENIXD BACKEND SCOPING: PaymentBackend trait -> phoenixd HTTP API mapping (createinvoice/payinvoice/getbalance/websocket settlement watch); INV-1 fee-cap seam vs ACINQ auto-liquidity fee model (operator-choice tradeoff table); per-backend backup (y4m.6/14 are fedimint-shaped) + reconcile stories. Same-invoice retry OK on phoenixd.
3. LNV2 NO-RETRY GAP across ALL bolt11 surfaces: refunds + operator sweeps + in-flight orders w/ persisted raw-bolt11 at cutover; options per surface: reject-at-intake, fresh-invoice-request workflow, or phoenixd routing.
4. LNV1 RETIREMENT PATH: freeze (done) -> new deployments lnv2-or-phoenixd -> migrate/retire lnv1 deployments -> DELETE the ADR-0017 machinery. Disposition table written AT RETIREMENT against the exact released lnv2 tag (never moving master); lnv2 API stability is its own gate.
5. VERSION-BUMP GATE: un-pinning the #8818 backport = the major v0.12 bump that brings lnv2 — an OUTPUT of this spec's timeline, never mechanical cleanup.
6. SEQUENCING: phoenixd backend has no upstream gate (can start first); lnv2 backend gates on a released API-stable lnv2.
Plan: ~/.gstack/projects/douglaz-lnrent/2026-07-16-master-design-next-steps-lnv2-85t.md

comments.author: master
comments.text: GRILL SESSION DECISIONS (2026-07-17, /grill-with-docs): (1) ADR-0018 written — lnv2 + phoenixd co-equal, lnv1 retired; 0012 partially superseded (pointer added). (2) SEED: the phoenixd seed DERIVES from the Operator seed (HKDF domain lnrent:phoenixd:v1 -> BIP39-encode -> supply at first start), preserving the one-backup promise; glossary updated. Spec must VERIFY: (a) phoenixd accepts an operator-supplied seed (pre-placed seed.dat or equivalent), (b) seed-only restore recovers funds via ACINQ peer storage (Phoenix-design assumption — confirm for phoenixd), and document the restore caveat like fedimint's invite caveat.

comments.author: master
comments.text: MAJOR CORRECTION (2026-07-17 grill): lnv2 is RELEASED — lnv2-client/common/server ship in v0.11.1, our current pin. No version gate, no bump. New sequencing: (1) build the lnv2 FedimintPayment impl NOW against the existing workspace; (2) phoenixd backend in parallel (ADR-0018 Q4: external service, config url+api_password, lnrent phoenixd-seed helper); (3) retarget the 7qc dogfood to lnv2; (4) DELETE lnv1 ASAP after: fedimint_backend.rs lnv1 paths + recover_pay_from_oplog + kum dead-op ledger + y4m.16 classification + y4m.15/19 GC + tests + fedimint-ln-client/-ln-common deps; pins return to upstream v0.11.1 tag (backport no longer needed). GREENFIELD: no migration, no operator-facing lnv1, ever. 85t closed as superseded (lessons ported, see its closing comment).

comments.author: master
comments.text: CLOSED AS RESOLVED (2026-07-17): the strategy questions this spec was going to answer were resolved by ADR-0018 + the /grill-with-docs session — no standalone strategy document (beads over documents; they do not rot). Decisions: lnv2 released in pinned v0.11.1, build now (lnrent-3d5); phoenixd external-service backend with derived seed (lnrent-xk3); 7qc dogfood retargeted to lnv2; lnv1 deleted after dogfood passes (lnrent-8ym), pins return to upstream tag; greenfield = no migration ever; one backend per Control node; one-seed backup preserved via lnrent:phoenixd:v1 derivation. Glossary: CONTEXT.md 'Payment backend' + updated 'Operator seed'. Open product decision flagged on lnrent-3d5: raw-bolt11 policy under lnv2's no-same-invoice-retry.

title: op-dispatch: serve in-flight RUNNING duplicates immediately (not defer-and-retry)
description:
## Outcome
When a buyer retries an op.request whose first attempt is still RUNNING, serve the in-flight invocation's result immediately instead of the current defer-and-retry (returning Err so the wrap is reprocessed on the next relay redelivery). The .20 contract (SPEC §5.1) wants a duplicate to get the cached OR in-flight result.

## Why
.20 deliberately scoped OUT an in-flight task registry for M1a (overengineering risk) — a concurrent-RUNNING duplicate returns Err (op_dispatch.rs), so it is not marked seen and is reprocessed later, by which time the first attempt has committed DONE/ERROR and the cached result is resent. Correct + safe (hook runs at most once), but a buyer retrying after a lost op.result gets silence until the next redelivery. Once .21 wires the daemon supervisor (which owns op task lifecycle), it can hold in-flight handles and serve a concurrent RUNNING duplicate immediately.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concurrent duplicate (same sender+request_id) arriving while the first hook is RUNNING receives the in-flight invocation's result (await/attach), not an Err-defer; the hook still runs exactly once.
- No regression to crash-recovery (orphaned RUNNING -> interrupted) or the DONE/ERROR cached-resend path.

## M1a-hardening review decision (2026-07-03): DEFER — correctly parked on .21
Reviewed as part of the M1a hardening set (with h18/k82/xjn). Confirmed NOT a hardening gap: current defer-and-retry is money-safe (hook runs at most once; a concurrent-RUNNING duplicate is Err-deferred, not marked seen, and served the cached DONE/ERROR result on the next redelivery). Serving in-flight duplicates immediately requires the in-flight task registry that .20 deliberately scoped OUT as overengineering; the supervisor that can hold in-flight handles arrives in .21. So this stays OPEN and blocked by .21 as a latency/UX enhancement, not reopened now.

title: Drain and retire the lnv1 dogfood wallet before deletion (manual, real money)
description:
Second-model audit B3. ADR-0018 says the only lnv1 wallet in the world (the author's dogfood) is drained by hand — this bead makes that a gate instead of a footnote. Before lnrent-8ym may start: drain or explicitly account for the dogfood wallet balance (lnrent sweep to a bolt11 from the successor wallet, or documented dust write-off); prove NO pending lnv1 payments/refunds/sweeps or open dogfood liabilities remain (lnrent money + refunds list + reconcile all clean); archive the recovery material (seed derivation note, federation invite, index db snapshot, drain evidence) so residual ecash is recoverable out-of-tree if anything was missed. MANUAL operator action with operator sign-off — an agent prepares the checklist and verifies the ledger surfaces, the operator moves the money.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wallet balance zero or explicitly written off; reconcile OK; refunds list empty; no open obligations.
- Recovery material archived (location recorded in the bead, not the repo).
- Operator confirms completion in a bead comment; only then does lnrent-8ym unblock.
close_reason: Written off. Exhaustive search (local FS + 4 kube contexts + all PVCs + ArgoCD) found no lnv1 lnrentd data dir; o6p's wallet location/seed/balance was never recorded, and a fedimint wallet needs the invite as well as the seed, so nothing is recoverable or quantifiable. No drain to perform. Recurrence owned by lnrent-rwv. Unblocks lnrent-8ym.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CONVERTED TO A WRITTEN-OFF AUDIT (2026-07-21, grill session, operator's call).

This bead assumed the lnv1 dogfood wallet still existed and could be drained. An exhaustive search
says it does not:

  LOCAL FILESYSTEM  no `lnrent.sqlite`, no `operator.seed`, no `fedimint.json` anywhere under
                    /home/master except /home/master/p/lnr7qc/data — which is the LNV2 wallet
                    created for the 7qc run on 2026-07-20, not the lnv1 dogfood.
  KUBERNETES        all four kubeconfig contexts (hetzner, do-nyc1, eu, k3d-wtf-staging): no
                    lnrent pod. No lnrent PVC in any namespace. No lnrent ArgoCD application
                    (17 apps, none lnrent). The only wallet-bearing PVC is `walletd-data`, which
                    belongs to the SEPARATE ~/p/fedimint-wallets project (image
                    registry.galtland.network/walletd/walletd:a72b697) — not lnrent.
  GIT/BEAD HISTORY  o6p closed as "fedimint go-live code-complete" with a live dogfood
                    (real buyer -> ecash -> DO droplet -> SSH -> cancel), but no bead or commit
                    records the wallet's location, seed, or final balance.

So the residual lnv1 ecash is UNRECOVERABLE and UNQUANTIFIABLE: the data dir is gone and nothing
recorded what it held. A fedimint wallet also cannot be recovered from a seed alone — it needs the
federation invite too (ADR-0018 / CONTEXT "Operator seed") — and neither survives.

WRITTEN OFF. There is no drain to perform and no recovery material to archive, because the material
was never captured. Amount: unknown, believed dust (the o6p dogfood ran at test scale, and by the
same accounting the whole 7qc run cost ~250 sat).

THE REAL LESSON, and it is not free: this bead was created to stop exactly this outcome, and it
still happened — because the practice it was protecting against ("dogfood creates a real wallet,
nobody records it, it disappears") was never written down anywhere an operator or agent would read
before the NEXT dogfood. The 7qc run then created five more untracked positions (~228 sat) before
anyone noticed the pattern. That recurrence is now owned by lnrent-rwv, which both consolidates
the current residuals AND requires every future dogfood bead to record its data dir, seed location,
federation invite, and final balance BEFORE the bead closes.

=> ccr closes as a write-off. lnrent-8ym is no longer blocked on it. Anyone reading this later:
the money is gone and the amount is unknown; the durable fix is lnrent-rwv, not this bead.

title: Exercise the late-order refund trigger live + measure claim latency L (7qc leg closed on a refuted premise)
description:
The late-order REFUND_DUE trigger is REACHABLE by a conforming payer in normal operation, and has never been exercised live. It was declared "black-box unreachable, therefore satisfied" when lnrent-7qc closed; that argument was refuted by adversarial review and withdrawn (see the correction comment on lnrent-7qc).

## The mechanism
`settled_at` for a LIVE lnv2 settlement is lnrent's wall clock at the moment the daemon OBSERVES the terminal state (`lnv2_backend.rs:1341`, after `await_receive_final` returns Claimed and after the `claimed_credit_msat` round-trip) — not the time the Buyer paid. The invoice `expires_at` is stamped at mint time (`lnv2_backend.rs:1015`, `INVOICE_EXPIRY_S = 3600`). The gate fires on `s.settled_at >= exp` (`capture.rs:184-189`).

So for L = claim-observation latency (gateway forward -> federation funds contract -> claim tx accepted -> credit decoded; seconds of consensus), a Buyer paying a VALID, UNEXPIRED bolt11 within L of the expiry boundary is refunded instead of provisioned. This is deliberate (`backends.rs:36-39`: "so a late LIVE payment refunds (capture's g5p gate) instead of being stamped just-in-window and wrongly provisioned (lnrent-zwk)") — the recovery path caps `recovered_settled_at` at `expires_at - 1`, the live path intentionally does not.

Second door: reconcile independently flips the invoice OPEN -> EXPIRED on its tick and releases the reservation; a settlement landing after that reaches the same RefundDue via capture's terminal arm.

## Why it matters
A real Buyer who pays in the last seconds of the hour gets their money back instead of a VM. That is the correct SAFE outcome, but it is a live money path — order paid, no service, refund owed — that has only ever run in unit tests. It is also a bad Buyer experience worth measuring: how wide is L in practice on a real federation?

## Work
- Exercise it live on the lnv2 backend: place an order, wait until just inside the expiry boundary, pay so the claim lands after it. The window is L wide (seconds), so script the timing and expect retries. Capture the same evidence 7qc required for the failed-provision trigger: daemon logs, the `refund_attempt` row reaching SENT with attempts=1, the sub reaching REFUNDED, and a buyer-side receipt.
- MEASURE L while doing it (time from `module ln pay` returning to the daemon's `settled_at`). That number is the real width of the mis-timed-order window and belongs in the operator docs.
- Consider whether a Buyer paying a valid bolt11 SHOULD lose the order at all, or whether the gate should compare against a deadline that accounts for L (e.g. stamp the invoice expiry shorter than the bolt11 expiry by ~L, so the bolt11 is already unpayable before the gate can fire). That is a product question, not just a test gap — record the answer either way.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The late-order trigger is demonstrated live at least once on lnv2, with evidence mapped one-to-one to the failed-provision evidence already on lnrent-7qc.
- L is measured on a real federation and recorded.
- A recorded decision on whether the expiry/gate relationship should change so a valid bolt11 cannot be paid into a refund, or an explicit note that the current behaviour is accepted.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CORRECTION (codex reviewer, PR #50): the "Consider whether..." option in this bead had the expiry
ordering BACKWARDS. To make a bolt11 UNPAYABLE before the capture gate can fire, the PAYMENT (bolt11)
expiry must be EARLIER than the capture/reservation cutoff by the claim-latency margin L — NOT the
invoice/capture expiry shorter than the bolt11 (which would leave a still-payable bolt11 after the
gate has begun firing, widening the paid-but-unserved window). So if this gate/expiry relationship is
changed, stamp the bolt11 expiry at (capture_cutoff - L), so the payer refuses the invoice before a
late settlement could reach the gate. The current design (bolt11 expiry == capture expiry) is what
makes the late-live-settlement reachable in the first place (see the mechanism above).

title: downtime credit: also credit SUSPENDED subs' retention/destroy deadline
description:
## Outcome
Extend downtime credit (lnrent-7fp.22) to SUBSCRIBED-then-SUSPENDED subs: a sub already SUSPENDED before an operator outage, whose retention/destroy deadline falls inside the outage window, must NOT be destroyed on restart — the buyer could not pay-to-resume while the operator was down. Shift its destroy cursor forward by the outage credit and keep capture's + order_intake's resumable boundary consistent with that shift.

## Why
.22 credits the ACTIVE renew_lead/suspend window (ADR-0005 revision's stated scope: 'renewal/suspend deadlines'). codex flagged (P1, data-loss) that a SUSPENDED sub destroyed for the operator's outage is the same class of buyer-harm — the buyer in retention grace couldn't pay-to-resume while down. Deferred from .22 because it is additional behavior beyond the ADR's stated scope and adds state-dependence to capture's refund gate (ACTIVE: max(pt,suspend_not_before)+retention_s; SUSPENDED: the credited destroy cursor). The .22 framework (suspend_not_before floor + credited-boundary honored in reconcile/capture/order_intake) is the foundation to build on.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A SUSPENDED sub whose destroy deadline fell in the outage is NOT destroyed on restart; its destroy is shifted by the outage credit; a pay-to-resume in the credited window resumes (capture + order_intake honor the shifted boundary), not refund/reject.
- An outage outside its retention window does not shift it.
close_reason: DONE + reviewer-verified: apply_restart_downtime_credit now also credits SUSPENDED subs' retention window lost to an operator outage (monotonic suspend_not_before floor, anti-resurrection B_old>last gate, gives back exactly the downtime overlap, aligns with capture's resumable cutoff). Spec docs/specs/downtime-credit-suspended.md (3-pass codex-review-loop); 8 new tests; rb-lite clean 1 round (both reviewers verified, not diff-blind). ACTIVE credit unchanged; RESUMING/CANCELLED skipped.

title: Revert the deployed gatewayd image to upstream once a v0.11.x ships the tpe 1-of-1 fix
description:
Two production pins currently exist ONLY because upstream fedimint has not yet shipped the tpe 1-of-1 fix. Both must be returned to upstream artifacts once it lands, and neither had an owner until now.

## Why the pins exist
`crypto/tpe`'s `lagrange_multipliers` panics when aggregating exactly ONE decryption share — i.e. on every aggregation on a single-guardian federation. The panic surfaces inside a client state-machine transition and kills the `sm-executor` task, after which the client keeps accepting work (intercepting HTLCs, writing TxSubmission state machines, logging "Finalized and submitting transaction") but submits nothing, silently, forever.

Upstream: issue #8837, fix PR #8838 (master, OPEN as of 2026-07-21), backport PR #8839 (releases/v0.11, DRAFT). No released v0.11.x contains it.

## The two pins
1. **Cargo dep pin** — the ten `fedimint-*` deps resolve from `douglaz/fedimint` branch `v0.11.1-pay-idempotency` @ 642f3ed8 rather than the upstream `v0.11.1` tag. After lnrent-8ym deletes lnv1, the #8818 half of that branch is dead weight and the tpe fix is the ONLY remaining reason for the fork (see the amendment comment on lnrent-8ym).
2. **Deployed gateway image** — `~/newmachine/remote-devops/k8s/argo/ostracoda-lnd-node/gatewayd.yaml` (commit b862ee5) pins `registry.galtland.network/library/gatewayd:v0.11.1-tpe-642f3ed8@sha256:168059f7...`, built from `douglaz/fedimint@642f3ed8`, replacing the upstream `fedimint/gatewayd:v0.11.1`. The in-file comment already says "REVERT to the upstream tag once a v0.11.x containing #8838 ships" — this bead is that reminder with an owner.

Note the gateway serves THREE mainnet federations (solomint, E-Cash Club, School of Sats), so its revert is a real operational change, not a dev-box edit.

## Trigger
A released fedimint v0.11.x whose changelog/tree contains the tpe fix (#8839 merged to releases/v0.11 and tagged). Watch #8838 and #8839.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Confirm the tpe fix is present in the target upstream tag by reading the tag's `crypto/tpe/src/lib.rs` (`git show <tag>:crypto/tpe/src/lib.rs`), NOT by trusting release notes.
- Cargo pins returned to that upstream tag; `cargo tree` shows no `douglaz/fedimint` source; workspace tests + clippy green with `--features fedimint` AND `--no-default-features`.
- Re-run `daemon/tests/lnv2_live.rs` against solomint (a 1-of-1 federation) on the upstream tag to prove the panic is actually gone in the shipped artifact, not just in the diff.
- gatewayd.yaml returned to the upstream image tag; after rollout, verify the gateway still serves all three federations (a receive on each) before closing.
- Remove the now-stale "keep the fork for the tpe fix" justification from Cargo.toml.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
NARROWED (2026-07-21, after adversarial review). This bead now owns ONLY the deployed gatewayd image
revert. The Cargo-pin half is VOID.

WHY: the tpe 1-of-1 panic is reachable only through `aggregate_dk_shares`, whose sole non-test caller
at v0.11.1 is `modules/fedimint-gwv2-client/src/receive_sm.rs:210` — the GATEWAY's lnv2 client.
lnrent does not depend on `fedimint-gwv2-client` (absent from both Cargo.toml and Cargo.lock);
`fedimint-lnv2-client` imports only `AggregateDecryptionKey`/`derive_agg_dk` and never aggregates
guardian shares. So lnrent's Cargo pin never needed the tpe fix, and lnrent-8ym can return those pins
to the upstream v0.11.1 tag on its own schedule (see the retraction comment on 8ym).

REMAINING SCOPE — the gateway only:
`~/newmachine/remote-devops/k8s/argo/ostracoda-lnd-node/gatewayd.yaml` (commit b862ee5) pins
`registry.galtland.network/library/gatewayd:v0.11.1-tpe-642f3ed8@sha256:168059f7...`, built from
douglaz/fedimint@642f3ed8, replacing upstream `fedimint/gatewayd:v0.11.1`. THIS pin is load-bearing:
without it the gateway's client panics on every decryption-share aggregation against a 1-of-1
federation, killing its sm-executor so it accepts HTLCs and submits nothing. Revert once a released
v0.11.x contains #8839.

Note the gateway serves THREE mainnet federations (solomint, E-Cash Club, School of Sats), so the
revert is a real operational change, not a dev-box edit.

ACCEPTANCE (revised):
- Confirm the fix is present in the target upstream tag by reading it (`git show <tag>:crypto/tpe/src/lib.rs`),
  not by trusting release notes.
- gatewayd.yaml returned to the upstream image tag; after rollout, verify a receive on EACH of the
  three federations before closing.
- Since the failure mode is silent (the gateway keeps accepting work and submits nothing), the
  post-rollout check must be a real receive, not a liveness probe.

comments.author: master
comments.text:
CLARIFICATION (2026-07-22): this bead's scope is UNCHANGED by the "keep the fork" decision on
lnrent-8ym, but the earlier framing needs a correction.

My 2026-07-21 narrowing note said e96 owns the gateway-image revert because "8ym handles the Cargo-pin
return." That half is now void: 8ym is NOT returning the Cargo pin — lnrent deliberately KEEPS the
douglaz/fedimint fork as standing patch infrastructure (see the 8ym keep-fork comment). So there is no
"Cargo-pin return" pending anywhere.

e96 remains exactly the GATEWAY IMAGE revert and nothing else:
`~/newmachine/remote-devops/k8s/argo/ostracoda-lnd-node/gatewayd.yaml` pins a self-built
gatewayd:v0.11.1-tpe-642f3ed8; revert it to an upstream gatewayd image once a released v0.11.x
contains the tpe fix (#8839). That pin IS load-bearing (the gateway's client DOES call the panicking
aggregate_dk_shares against a 1-of-1 fed), unlike lnrent's own deps.

Net: lnrent's Cargo pins stay on the fork indefinitely (by choice); only the GATEWAY IMAGE is tracked
